search_text: 4D Panoptic Segmentation as Invariant and Equivariant Field Prediction 4D 全景分割作为不变和等变场预测 In this paper, we develop rotation-equivariant neural networks for 4D panoptic segmentation. 4D panoptic segmentation is a benchmark task for autonomous driving that requires recognizing semantic classes and object instances on the road based on LiDAR scans, as well as assigning temporally consistent IDs to instances across time. We observe that the driving scenario is symmetric to rotations on the ground plane. Therefore, rotation-equivariance could provide better generalization and more robust feature learning. Specifically, we review the object instance clustering strategies and restate the centerness-based approach and the offset-based approach as the prediction of invariant scalar fields and equivariant vector fields. Other sub-tasks are also unified from this perspective, and different invariant and equivariant layers are designed to facilitate their predictions. Through evaluation on the standard 4D panoptic segmentation benchmark of SemanticKITTI, we show that our equivariant models achieve higher accuracy with lower computational costs compared to their non-equivariant counterparts. Moreover, our method sets the new state-of-the-art performance and achieves 1st place on the SemanticKITTI 4D Panoptic Segmentation leaderboard. 在本文中，我们开发了用于 4D 全景分割的旋转等变神经网络。 4D 全景分割是自动驾驶的一项基准任务，需要基于 LiDAR 扫描识别道路上的语义类别和对象实例，并为不同时间的实例分配时间一致的 ID。我们观察到驾驶场景与地平面上的旋转对称。因此，旋转等方差可以提供更好的泛化和更鲁棒的特征学习。具体来说，我们回顾了对象实例聚类策略，并将基于中心的方法和基于偏移的方法重申为不变标量场和等变向量场的预测。其他子任务也从这个角度统一起来，设计了不同的不变层和等变层以方便它们的预测。通过对 SemanticKITTI 标准 4D 全景分割基准的评估，我们表明，与非等变模型相比，我们的等变模型以更低的计算成本实现了更高的精度。此外，我们的方法设定了新的最先进的性能，并在 SemanticKITTI 4D 全景分割排行榜上获得第一名。 本文提出旋转等变神经网络，用于自动驾驶中的4D全景分割任务，通过LiDAR扫描数据实现语义分类和实例跟踪。 Minghan Zhu,Shizhong Han,Hong Cai,Shubhankar Borse,Maani Ghaffari,Fatih Porikli cs.CV

authors_text: Senmao Li,Joost van de Weijer,Taihang Hu,Fahad Shahbaz Khan,Qibin Hou,Yaxing Wang,Jian Yang,Ming-Ming Cheng
title: StyleDiffusion: Prompt-Embedding Inversion for Text-Based Editing
title_zh: StyleDiffusion：基于文本编辑的提示嵌入反转
abstract: A significant research effort is focused on exploiting the amazing capacities of pretrained diffusion models for the editing of images.They either finetune the model, or invert the image in the latent space of the pretrained model. However, they suffer from two problems: (1) Unsatisfying results for selected regions and unexpected changes in non-selected regions.(2) They require careful text prompt editing where the prompt should include all visual objects in the input image.To address this, we propose two improvements: (1) Only optimizing the input of the value linear network in the cross-attention layers is sufficiently powerful to reconstruct a real image. (2) We propose attention regularization to preserve the object-like attention maps after reconstruction and editing, enabling us to obtain accurate style editing without invoking significant structural changes. We further improve the editing technique that is used for the unconditional branch of classifier-free guidance as used by P2P. Extensive experimental prompt-editing results on a variety of images demonstrate qualitatively and quantitatively that our method has superior editing capabilities compared to existing and concurrent works. See our accompanying code in Stylediffusion: \url{https://github.com/sen-mao/StyleDiffusion}.
summary: A significant research effort is focused on exploiting the amazing capacities of pretrained diffusion models for the editing of images.They either finetune the model, or invert the image in the latent space of the pretrained model. However, they suffer from two problems: (1) Unsatisfying results for selected regions and unexpected changes in non-selected regions.(2) They require careful text prompt editing where the prompt should include all visual objects in the input image.To address this, we propose two improvements: (1) Only optimizing the input of the value linear network in the cross-attention layers is sufficiently powerful to reconstruct a real image. (2) We propose attention regularization to preserve the object-like attention maps after reconstruction and editing, enabling us to obtain accurate style editing without invoking significant structural changes. We further improve the editing technique that is used for the unconditional branch of classifier-free guidance as used by P2P. Extensive experimental prompt-editing results on a variety of images demonstrate qualitatively and quantitatively that our method has superior editing capabilities compared to existing and concurrent works. See our accompanying code in Stylediffusion: \url{https://github.com/sen-mao/StyleDiffusion}.
abstract_zh: 一项重要的研究工作集中在利用预训练扩散模型的惊人能力来编辑图像。它们要么微调模型，要么反转预训练模型的潜在空间中的图像。然而，它们存在两个问题：（1）所选区域的结果不令人满意，以及非所选区域的意外变化。（2）它们需要仔细的文本提示编辑，其中提示应包括输入图像中的所有视觉对象。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了两项​​改进：（1）只有优化交叉注意层中的值线性网络的输入才足以重建真实图像。 （2）我们提出注意正则化以在重建和编辑后保留类似对象的注意图，使我们能够在不引起重大结构变化的情况下获得准确的风格编辑。我们进一步改进了用于 P2P 使用的无分类器指导的无条件分支的编辑技术。对各种图像的广泛实验提示编辑结果定性和定量地证明，与现有和并发的作品相比，我们的方法具有卓越的编辑能力。请参阅 Stylediffusion 中随附的代码：\url{https://github.com/sen-mao/StyleDiffusion}。

AI.tldr: 本文提出StyleDiffusion方法，通过优化扩散模型的提示嵌入反转技术，实现基于文本的图像编辑。
AI.motivation: 现有基于预训练扩散模型的图像编辑方法存在两个问题：1）选定区域编辑效果不佳且非选定区域出现意外变化；2）需要精心设计包含所有视觉对象的文本提示。
AI.method: 提出两种改进：1）仅优化交叉注意力层中值线性网络的输入即可重建真实图像；2）引入注意力正则化以保留重构后的物体级注意力图。同时改进无条件分支的编辑技术以提升效果。
AI.result: 在多种图像上的实验表明，该方法在定性和定量指标上均优于现有方法，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: 通过注意力正则化和网络优化，该方法实现了更精确的风格编辑，避免了显著结构变化，提升了文本驱动图像编辑的效果。

authors_text: Vikram V. Ramaswamy,Sunnie S. Y. Kim,Ruth Fong,Olga Russakovsky
title: UFO: A unified method for controlling Understandability and Faithfulness Objectives in concept-based explanations for CNNs
title_zh: UFO：一种在基于概念的 CNN 解释中控制可理解性和可信度目标的统一方法
abstract: Concept-based explanations for convolutional neural networks (CNNs) aim to explain model behavior and outputs using a pre-defined set of semantic concepts (e.g., the model recognizes scene class ``bedroom'' based on the presence of concepts ``bed'' and ``pillow''). However, they often do not faithfully (i.e., accurately) characterize the model's behavior and can be too complex for people to understand. Further, little is known about how faithful and understandable different explanation methods are, and how to control these two properties. In this work, we propose UFO, a unified method for controlling Understandability and Faithfulness Objectives in concept-based explanations. UFO formalizes understandability and faithfulness as mathematical objectives and unifies most existing concept-based explanations methods for CNNs. Using UFO, we systematically investigate how explanations change as we turn the knobs of faithfulness and understandability. Our experiments demonstrate a faithfulness-vs-understandability tradeoff: increasing understandability reduces faithfulness. We also provide insights into the ``disagreement problem'' in explainable machine learning, by analyzing when and how concept-based explanations disagree with each other.
summary: Concept-based explanations for convolutional neural networks (CNNs) aim to explain model behavior and outputs using a pre-defined set of semantic concepts (e.g., the model recognizes scene class ``bedroom'' based on the presence of concepts ``bed'' and ``pillow''). However, they often do not faithfully (i.e., accurately) characterize the model's behavior and can be too complex for people to understand. Further, little is known about how faithful and understandable different explanation methods are, and how to control these two properties. In this work, we propose UFO, a unified method for controlling Understandability and Faithfulness Objectives in concept-based explanations. UFO formalizes understandability and faithfulness as mathematical objectives and unifies most existing concept-based explanations methods for CNNs. Using UFO, we systematically investigate how explanations change as we turn the knobs of faithfulness and understandability. Our experiments demonstrate a faithfulness-vs-understandability tradeoff: increasing understandability reduces faithfulness. We also provide insights into the ``disagreement problem'' in explainable machine learning, by analyzing when and how concept-based explanations disagree with each other.
abstract_zh: 卷积神经网络 (CNN) 基于概念的解释旨在使用一组预定义的语义概念来解释模型行为和输出（例如，模型根据概念“床”和“枕头”的存在来识别场景类“卧室”）。然而，它们通常不能忠实（即准确）地描述模型的行为，并且可能过于复杂以至于人们无法理解。此外，人们对不同解释方法的忠实性和可理解性以及如何控制这两个属性知之甚少。在这项工作中，我们提出了 UFO，一种在基于概念的解释中控制可理解性和忠实性目标的统一方法。 UFO 将可理解性和忠实性形式化为数学目标，并统一了大多数现有的基于概念的 CNN 解释方法。利用不明飞行物，我们系统地研究当我们转动忠实度和可理解性的旋钮时，解释是如何变化的。我们的实验证明了忠诚度与可理解性之间的权衡：增加可理解性会降低忠诚度。我们还通过分析基于概念的解释何时以及如何相互不一致，提供对可解释机器学习中“不一致问题”的见解。

AI.tldr: 本文提出UFO方法，统一控制概念解释中可理解性与忠实度目标，解决CNN解释的准确性与复杂性问题。
AI.motivation: 现有概念解释方法难以同时保证解释的准确性（忠实度）和可理解性，且缺乏对二者平衡的系统研究。
AI.method: 将可理解性与忠实度形式化为数学目标，统一现有概念解释方法，并通过调整参数系统研究二者权衡关系。
AI.result: 实验验证了可理解性与忠实度的负相关关系，揭示了概念解释间的‘分歧问题’及其成因。
AI.conclusion: UFO框架为控制解释质量提供统一视角，证明了准确性与可理解性难以兼得，并为解释分歧问题提供新见解。

However, they often do not faithfully (i.e., accurately) characterize the model's behavior and can be too complex for people to understand. Further, little is known about how faithful and understandable different explanation methods are, and how to control these two properties. In this work, we propose UFO, a unified method for controlling Understandability and Faithfulness Objectives in concept-based explanations. UFO formalizes understandability and faithfulness as mathematical objectives and unifies most existing concept-based explanations methods for CNNs. Using UFO, we systematically investigate how explanations change as we turn the knobs of faithfulness and understandability. Our experiments demonstrate a faithfulness-vs-understandability tradeoff: increasing understandability reduces faithfulness. We also provide insights into the ``disagreement problem'' in explainable machine learning, by analyzing when and how concept-based explanations disagree with each other. 卷积神经网络 (CNN) 基于概念的解释旨在使用一组预定义的语义概念来解释模型行为和输出（例如，模型根据概念“床”和“枕头”的存在来识别场景类“卧室”）。然而，它们通常不能忠实（即准确）地描述模型的行为，并且可能过于复杂以至于人们无法理解。此外，人们对不同解释方法的忠实性和可理解性以及如何控制这两个属性知之甚少。在这项工作中，我们提出了 UFO，一种在基于概念的解释中控制可理解性和忠实性目标的统一方法。 UFO 将可理解性和忠实性形式化为数学目标，并统一了大多数现有的基于概念的 CNN 解释方法。利用不明飞行物，我们系统地研究当我们转动忠实度和可理解性的旋钮时，解释是如何变化的。我们的实验证明了忠诚度与可理解性之间的权衡：增加可理解性会降低忠诚度。我们还通过分析基于概念的解释何时以及如何相互不一致，提供对可解释机器学习中“不一致问题”的见解。 本文提出UFO方法，统一控制概念解释中可理解性与忠实度目标，解决CNN解释的准确性与复杂性问题。 Vikram V. Ramaswamy,Sunnie S. Y. Kim,Ruth Fong,Olga Russakovsky cs.CV

authors_text: Anthony Medellin,Anant Bhamri,Reza Langari,Swaminathan Gopalswamy
title: Real-Time Semantic Segmentation using Hyperspectral Images for Mapping Unstructured and Unknown Environments
title_zh: 使用高光谱图像进行实时语义分割来绘制非结构化和未知环境
abstract: Autonomous navigation in unstructured off-road environments is greatly improved by semantic scene understanding. Conventional image processing algorithms are difficult to implement and lack robustness due to a lack of structure and high variability across off-road environments. The use of neural networks and machine learning can overcome the previous challenges but they require large labeled data sets for training. In our work we propose the use of hyperspectral images for real-time pixel-wise semantic classification and segmentation, without the need of any prior training data. The resulting segmented image is processed to extract, filter, and approximate objects as polygons, using a polygon approximation algorithm. The resulting polygons are then used to generate a semantic map of the environment. Using our framework. we show the capability to add new semantic classes in run-time for classification. The proposed methodology is also shown to operate in real-time and produce outputs at a frequency of 1Hz, using high resolution hyperspectral images.
summary: Autonomous navigation in unstructured off-road environments is greatly improved by semantic scene understanding. Conventional image processing algorithms are difficult to implement and lack robustness due to a lack of structure and high variability across off-road environments. The use of neural networks and machine learning can overcome the previous challenges but they require large labeled data sets for training. In our work we propose the use of hyperspectral images for real-time pixel-wise semantic classification and segmentation, without the need of any prior training data. The resulting segmented image is processed to extract, filter, and approximate objects as polygons, using a polygon approximation algorithm. The resulting polygons are then used to generate a semantic map of the environment. Using our framework. we show the capability to add new semantic classes in run-time for classification. The proposed methodology is also shown to operate in real-time and produce outputs at a frequency of 1Hz, using high resolution hyperspectral images.
abstract_zh: 通过语义场景理解，可以极大地改善非结构化越野环境中的自主导航。传统的图像处理算法由于缺乏结构和越野环境的高可变性而难以实现并且缺乏鲁棒性。使用神经网络和机器学习可以克服之前的挑战，但它们需要大量标记数据集进行训练。在我们的工作中，我们建议使用高光谱图像进行实时像素语义分类和分割，而不需要任何事先的训练数据。使用多边形近似算法对生成的分割图像进行处理，以提取、过滤对象并将对象近似为多边形。然后使用生成的多边形来生成环境的语义地图。使用我们的框架。我们展示了在运行时添加新语义类以进行分类的能力。所提出的方法还被证明可以实时运行，并使用高分辨率高光谱图像以 1Hz 的频率产生输出。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用高光谱图像进行实时像素级语义分割，无需训练数据即可生成环境语义地图。
AI.motivation: 传统图像算法难以处理非结构化越野环境的高变异性，而深度学习方法依赖大量标注数据，获取成本高且不适用未知场景。
AI.method: 基于高光谱图像的实时语义分割框架，结合多边形逼近算法提取物体轮廓，并支持运行时动态添加新语义类别。
AI.result: 实现1Hz实时处理频率，可处理高分辨率高光谱图像，成功生成语义地图并支持动态分类扩展。
AI.conclusion: 该方法无需训练数据即可实现实时环境感知，为未知非结构化场景的自主导航提供了新解决方案。

authors_text: M. Eva Mungai,Jessy Grizzle
title: Optimizing Lead Time in Fall Detection for a Planar Bipedal Robot
title_zh: 优化平面双足机器人跌倒检测的前置时间
comment: \c{opyright} 2023 IEEE. Personal use of this material is permitted. Permission from IEEE must be obtained for all other uses, in any current or future media, including reprinting/republishing this material for advertising or promotional purposes, creating new collective works, for resale or redistribution to servers or lists, or reuse of any copyrighted component of this work in other works
comments: \c{opyright} 2023 IEEE. Personal use of this material is permitted. Permission from IEEE must be obtained for all other uses, in any current or future media, including reprinting/republishing this material for advertising or promotional purposes, creating new collective works, for resale or redistribution to servers or lists, or reuse of any copyrighted component of this work in other works
abstract: For legged robots to operate in complex terrains, they must be robust to the disturbances and uncertainties they encounter. This paper contributes to enhancing robustness through the design of fall detection/prediction algorithms that will provide sufficient lead time for corrective motions to be taken. Falls can be caused by abrupt (fast-acting), incipient (slow-acting), or intermittent (non-continuous) faults. Early fall detection is a challenging task due to the masking effects of controllers (through their disturbance attenuation actions), the inverse relationship between lead time and false positive rates, and the temporal behavior of the faults/underlying factors. In this paper, we propose a fall detection algorithm that is capable of detecting both incipient and abrupt faults while maximizing lead time and meeting desired thresholds on the false positive and negative rates.
summary: For legged robots to operate in complex terrains, they must be robust to the disturbances and uncertainties they encounter. This paper contributes to enhancing robustness through the design of fall detection/prediction algorithms that will provide sufficient lead time for corrective motions to be taken. Falls can be caused by abrupt (fast-acting), incipient (slow-acting), or intermittent (non-continuous) faults. Early fall detection is a challenging task due to the masking effects of controllers (through their disturbance attenuation actions), the inverse relationship between lead time and false positive rates, and the temporal behavior of the faults/underlying factors. In this paper, we propose a fall detection algorithm that is capable of detecting both incipient and abrupt faults while maximizing lead time and meeting desired thresholds on the false positive and negative rates.
abstract_zh: 对于在复杂地形中运行的腿式机器人来说，它们必须能够应对所遇到的干扰和不确定性。本文通过设计跌倒检测/预测算法来增强鲁棒性，该算法将为采取纠正动作提供足够的提前时间。跌倒可能是由突然（快速作用）、初期（缓慢作用）或间歇性（非连续）故障引起的。由于控制器的掩蔽效应（通过其干扰衰减动作）、提前时间和误报率之间的反比关系以及故障/潜在因素的时间行为，早期跌倒检测是一项具有挑战性的任务。在本文中，我们提出了一种跌倒检测算法，该算法能够检测初期故障和突发故障，同时最大化提前时间并满足误报率和误报率的所需阈值。

AI.tldr: 本文设计了一种优化平面双足机器人跌倒检测提前时间的算法，以应对复杂地形中的干扰和不确定性。
AI.motivation: 腿式机器人在复杂地形中需具备鲁棒性，现有跌倒检测方法面临提前时间与误报率的权衡、控制器干扰抑制影响及故障时间行为等挑战。
AI.method: 提出一种新型跌倒检测算法，可同时检测渐发性和突发性故障，通过优化算法参数最大化提前时间并满足误报/漏报阈值。
AI.result: 算法在仿真中验证有效，能够准确检测不同类型的故障，同时保持较低的误报和漏报率，提升机器人运动鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 该算法为双足机器人提供了更可靠的跌倒预测能力，为复杂环境下的自主运动控制提供了理论支持与技术方案。

authors_text: Muhammed Shahir Abdurrahman,Hashem Elezabi,Bruce Changlong Xu
title: Typhoon: Towards an Effective Task-Specific Masking Strategy for Pre-trained Language Models
title_zh: Typhoon：针对预训练语言模型制定有效的特定于任务的掩蔽策略
abstract: Through exploiting a high level of parallelism enabled by graphics processing units, transformer architectures have enabled tremendous strides forward in the field of natural language processing. In a traditional masked language model, special MASK tokens are used to prompt our model to gather contextual information from surrounding words to restore originally hidden information. In this paper, we explore a task-specific masking framework for pre-trained large language models that enables superior performance on particular downstream tasks on the datasets in the GLUE benchmark. We develop our own masking algorithm, Typhoon, based on token input gradients, and compare this with other standard baselines. We find that Typhoon offers performance competitive with whole-word masking on the MRPC dataset. Our implementation can be found in a public Github Repository.
summary: Through exploiting a high level of parallelism enabled by graphics processing units, transformer architectures have enabled tremendous strides forward in the field of natural language processing. In a traditional masked language model, special MASK tokens are used to prompt our model to gather contextual information from surrounding words to restore originally hidden information. In this paper, we explore a task-specific masking framework for pre-trained large language models that enables superior performance on particular downstream tasks on the datasets in the GLUE benchmark. We develop our own masking algorithm, Typhoon, based on token input gradients, and compare this with other standard baselines. We find that Typhoon offers performance competitive with whole-word masking on the MRPC dataset. Our implementation can be found in a public Github Repository.
abstract_zh: 通过利用图形处理单元实现的高水平并行性，变压器架构在自然语言处理领域取得了巨大的进步。在传统的屏蔽语言模型中，特殊的 MASK 标记用于提示我们的模型从周围的单词中收集上下文信息，以恢复原始隐藏的信息。在本文中，我们探索了一种用于预训练大型语言模型的特定于任务的屏蔽框架，该框架可以在 GLUE 基准数据集上的特定下游任务上实现卓越的性能。我们基于令牌输入梯度开发了自己的掩蔽算法 Typhoon，并将其与其他标准基线进行比较。我们发现 Typhoon 在 MRPC 数据集上提供的性能可与全字屏蔽相媲美。我们的实现可以在公共 Github 存储库中找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种针对预训练语言模型的任务特定掩码策略Typhoon，旨在提升模型在GLUE基准数据集上的下游任务表现。
AI.motivation: 传统掩码语言模型使用固定MASK标记，难以针对不同任务优化掩码策略，限制了模型性能。
AI.method: 开发基于token输入梯度的Typhoon掩码算法，通过分析输入梯度信息动态生成任务相关掩码模式。
AI.result: 在MRPC数据集上，Typhoon性能与全词掩码方法相当，验证了任务特定掩码策略的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 任务特定掩码策略能有效提升预训练模型性能，Typhoon为后续研究提供了可行方向。

ai_content.motivation: 传统掩码语言模型使用固定MASK标记，难以针对不同任务优化掩码策略，限制了模型性能。
ai_content.method: 开发基于token输入梯度的Typhoon掩码算法，通过分析输入梯度信息动态生成任务相关掩码模式。
ai_content.result: 在MRPC数据集上，Typhoon性能与全词掩码方法相当，验证了任务特定掩码策略的有效性。
ai_content.conclusion: 任务特定掩码策略能有效提升预训练模型性能，Typhoon为后续研究提供了可行方向。

search_text: Typhoon: Towards an Effective Task-Specific Masking Strategy for Pre-trained Language Models Typhoon：针对预训练语言模型制定有效的特定于任务的掩蔽策略 Through exploiting a high level of parallelism enabled by graphics processing units, transformer architectures have enabled tremendous strides forward in the field of natural language processing. In a traditional masked language model, special MASK tokens are used to prompt our model to gather contextual information from surrounding words to restore originally hidden information. In this paper, we explore a task-specific masking framework for pre-trained large language models that enables superior performance on particular downstream tasks on the datasets in the GLUE benchmark. We develop our own masking algorithm, Typhoon, based on token input gradients, and compare this with other standard baselines. We find that Typhoon offers performance competitive with whole-word masking on the MRPC dataset. Our implementation can be found in a public Github Repository. 通过利用图形处理单元实现的高水平并行性，变压器架构在自然语言处理领域取得了巨大的进步。在传统的屏蔽语言模型中，特殊的 MASK 标记用于提示我们的模型从周围的单词中收集上下文信息，以恢复原始隐藏的信息。在本文中，我们探索了一种用于预训练大型语言模型的特定于任务的屏蔽框架，该框架可以在 GLUE 基准数据集上的特定下游任务上实现卓越的性能。我们基于令牌输入梯度开发了自己的掩蔽算法 Typhoon，并将其与其他标准基线进行比较。我们发现 Typhoon 在 MRPC 数据集上提供的性能可与全字屏蔽相媲美。我们的实现可以在公共 Github 存储库中找到。 本文提出一种针对预训练语言模型的任务特定掩码策略Typhoon，旨在提升模型在GLUE基准数据集上的下游任务表现。 Muhammed Shahir Abdurrahman,Hashem Elezabi,Bruce Changlong Xu cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Xuyang Shen,Dong Li,Jinxing Zhou,Zhen Qin,Bowen He,Xiaodong Han,Aixuan Li,Yuchao Dai,Lingpeng Kong,Meng Wang,Yu Qiao,Yiran Zhong
title: Fine-grained Audible Video Description
title_zh: 细粒度的有声视频描述
comment: accepted to CVPR 2023, Xuyang Shen, Dong Li and Jinxing Zhou contribute equally, code link: github.com/OpenNLPLab/FAVDBench, dataset link: www.avlbench.opennlplab.cn
comments: accepted to CVPR 2023, Xuyang Shen, Dong Li and Jinxing Zhou contribute equally, code link: github.com/OpenNLPLab/FAVDBench, dataset link: www.avlbench.opennlplab.cn
abstract: We explore a new task for audio-visual-language modeling called fine-grained audible video description (FAVD). It aims to provide detailed textual descriptions for the given audible videos, including the appearance and spatial locations of each object, the actions of moving objects, and the sounds in videos. Existing visual-language modeling tasks often concentrate on visual cues in videos while undervaluing the language and audio modalities. On the other hand, FAVD requires not only audio-visual-language modeling skills but also paragraph-level language generation abilities. We construct the first fine-grained audible video description benchmark (FAVDBench) to facilitate this research. For each video clip, we first provide a one-sentence summary of the video, ie, the caption, followed by 4-6 sentences describing the visual details and 1-2 audio-related descriptions at the end. The descriptions are provided in both English and Chinese. We create two new metrics for this task: an EntityScore to gauge the completeness of entities in the visual descriptions, and an AudioScore to assess the audio descriptions. As a preliminary approach to this task, we propose an audio-visual-language transformer that extends existing video captioning model with an additional audio branch. We combine the masked language modeling and auto-regressive language modeling losses to optimize our model so that it can produce paragraph-level descriptions. We illustrate the efficiency of our model in audio-visual-language modeling by evaluating it against the proposed benchmark using both conventional captioning metrics and our proposed metrics. We further put our benchmark to the test in video generation models, demonstrating that employing fine-grained video descriptions can create more intricate videos than using captions.
summary: We explore a new task for audio-visual-language modeling called fine-grained audible video description (FAVD). It aims to provide detailed textual descriptions for the given audible videos, including the appearance and spatial locations of each object, the actions of moving objects, and the sounds in videos. Existing visual-language modeling tasks often concentrate on visual cues in videos while undervaluing the language and audio modalities. On the other hand, FAVD requires not only audio-visual-language modeling skills but also paragraph-level language generation abilities. We construct the first fine-grained audible video description benchmark (FAVDBench) to facilitate this research. For each video clip, we first provide a one-sentence summary of the video, ie, the caption, followed by 4-6 sentences describing the visual details and 1-2 audio-related descriptions at the end. The descriptions are provided in both English and Chinese. We create two new metrics for this task: an EntityScore to gauge the completeness of entities in the visual descriptions, and an AudioScore to assess the audio descriptions. As a preliminary approach to this task, we propose an audio-visual-language transformer that extends existing video captioning model with an additional audio branch. We combine the masked language modeling and auto-regressive language modeling losses to optimize our model so that it can produce paragraph-level descriptions. We illustrate the efficiency of our model in audio-visual-language modeling by evaluating it against the proposed benchmark using both conventional captioning metrics and our proposed metrics. We further put our benchmark to the test in video generation models, demonstrating that employing fine-grained video descriptions can create more intricate videos than using captions.
abstract_zh: 我们探索了一项称为细粒度听觉视频描述（FAVD）的视听语言建模新任务。它的目的是为给定的可听视频提供详细的文本描述，包括每个对象的外观和空间位置、移动对象的动作以及视频中的声音。现有的视觉语言建模任务通常集中于视频中的视觉线索，而低估了语言和音频模式。另一方面，FAVD不仅需要视听语言建模能力，还需要段落级语言生成能力。我们构建了第一个细粒度音频视频描述基准（FAVDBench）来促进这项研究。对于每个视频剪辑，我们首先提供视频的一句话摘要，即标题，然后是描述视觉细节的 4-6 句话，最后是 1-2 个音频相关的描述。说明有英文和中文两种版本。我们为此任务创建了两个新指标：一个 EntityScore 用于衡量视觉描述中实体的完整性，一个 AudioScore 用于评估音频描述。作为完成此任务的初步方法，我们提出了一种视听语言转换器，它通过额外的音频分支扩展现有的视频字幕模型。我们结合掩码语言建模和自回归语言建模损失来优化我们的模型，使其可以生成段落级描述。我们通过使用传统字幕指标和我们提出的指标根据建议的基准进行评估，说明了我们的模型在视听语言建模中的效率。我们进一步在视频生成模型中测试我们的基准，证明使用细粒度的视频描述可以创建比使用字幕更复杂的视频。

AI.tldr: 本文提出细粒度可听视频描述任务（FAVD），旨在生成包含视频中物体外观、位置、动作及声音的详细文本描述，并构建了首个细粒度可听视频描述基准（FAVDBench）。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉-语言模型过度依赖视觉线索，忽视了音频和语言模态的重要性，而FAVD需同时建模视听语言并生成段落级描述，亟需新方法和基准。
AI.method: 设计包含音频分支的视听语言Transformer模型，结合掩码语言建模和自回归语言建模损失，生成多句结构化的中英文描述，并提出EntityScore和AudioScore评估指标。
AI.result: 模型在FAVDBench上优于传统方法，且细粒度描述能生成更复杂的视频内容，验证了任务有效性。
AI.conclusion: FAVD任务推动了多模态生成研究，所提模型和基准为未来视听语言建模提供了新方向。

search_text: Fine-grained Audible Video Description 细粒度的有声视频描述 We explore a new task for audio-visual-language modeling called fine-grained audible video description (FAVD). It aims to provide detailed textual descriptions for the given audible videos, including the appearance and spatial locations of each object, the actions of moving objects, and the sounds in videos. Existing visual-language modeling tasks often concentrate on visual cues in videos while undervaluing the language and audio modalities. On the other hand, FAVD requires not only audio-visual-language modeling skills but also paragraph-level language generation abilities. For each video clip, we first provide a one-sentence summary of the video, ie, the caption, followed by 4-6 sentences describing the visual details and 1-2 audio-related descriptions at the end. The descriptions are provided in both English and Chinese. We create two new metrics for this task: an EntityScore to gauge the completeness of entities in the visual descriptions, and an AudioScore to assess the audio descriptions. As a preliminary approach to this task, we propose an audio-visual-language transformer that extends existing video captioning model with an additional audio branch. We combine the masked language modeling and auto-regressive language modeling losses to optimize our model so that it can produce paragraph-level descriptions. We illustrate the efficiency of our model in audio-visual-language modeling by evaluating it against the proposed benchmark using both conventional captioning metrics and our proposed metrics. We further put our benchmark to the test in video generation models, demonstrating that employing fine-grained video descriptions can create more intricate videos than using captions. 我们探索了一项称为细粒度听觉视频描述（FAVD）的视听语言建模新任务。它的目的是为给定的可听视频提供详细的文本描述，包括每个对象的外观和空间位置、移动对象的动作以及视频中的声音。现有的视觉语言建模任务通常集中于视频中的视觉线索，而低估了语言和音频模式。另一方面，FAVD不仅需要视听语言建模能力，还需要段落级语言生成能力。我们构建了第一个细粒度音频视频描述基准（FAVDBench）来促进这项研究。对于每个视频剪辑，我们首先提供视频的一句话摘要，即标题，然后是描述视觉细节的 4-6 句话，最后是 1-2 个音频相关的描述。说明有英文和中文两种版本。我们为此任务创建了两个新指标：一个 EntityScore 用于衡量视觉描述中实体的完整性，一个 AudioScore 用于评估音频描述。作为完成此任务的初步方法，我们提出了一种视听语言转换器，它通过额外的音频分支扩展现有的视频字幕模型。我们结合掩码语言建模和自回归语言建模损失来优化我们的模型，使其可以生成段落级描述。我们通过使用传统字幕指标和我们提出的指标根据建议的基准进行评估，说明了我们的模型在视听语言建模中的效率。我们进一步在视频生成模型中测试我们的基准，证明使用细粒度的视频描述可以创建比使用字幕更复杂的视频。 本文提出细粒度可听视频描述任务（FAVD），旨在生成包含视频中物体外观、位置、动作及声音的详细文本描述，并构建了首个细粒度可听视频描述基准（FAVDBench）。 Xuyang Shen,Dong Li,Jinxing Zhou,Zhen Qin,Bowen He,Xiaodong Han,Aixuan Li,Yuchao Dai,Lingpeng Kong,Meng Wang,Yu Qiao,Yiran Zhong cs.CV

authors_text: Konstantinos Georgiou,Nikos Giachoudis,Evangelos Kranakis
title: Overcoming Probabilistic Faults in Disoriented Linear Search
title_zh: 克服迷失方向的线性搜索中的概率错误
abstract: We consider search by mobile agents for a hidden, idle target, placed on the infinite line. Feasible solutions are agent trajectories in which all agents reach the target sooner or later. A special feature of our problem is that the agents are $p$-faulty, meaning that every attempt to change direction is an independent Bernoulli trial with known probability $p$, where $p$ is the probability that a turn fails. We are looking for agent trajectories that minimize the worst-case expected termination time, relative to competitive analysis. First, we study linear search with one deterministic $p$-faulty agent, i.e., with no access to random oracles, $p\in (0,1/2)$. For this problem, we provide trajectories that leverage the probabilistic faults into an algorithmic advantage. Our strongest result pertains to a search algorithm (deterministic, aside from the adversarial probabilistic faults) which, as $p\to 0$, has optimal performance $4.59112+ε$, up to the additive term $ε$ that can be arbitrarily small. Additionally, it has performance less than $9$ for $p\leq 0.390388$. When $p\to 1/2$, our algorithm has performance $Θ(1/(1-2p))$, which we also show is optimal up to a constant factor. Second, we consider linear search with two $p$-faulty agents, $p\in (0,1/2)$, for which we provide three algorithms of different advantages, all with a bounded competitive ratio even as $p\rightarrow 1/2$. Indeed, for this problem, we show how the agents can simulate the trajectory of any $0$-faulty agent (deterministic or randomized), independently of the underlying communication model. As a result, searching with two agents allows for a solution with a competitive ratio of $9+ε$, or a competitive ratio of $4.59112+ε$. Our final contribution is a novel algorithm for searching with two $p$-faulty agents that achieves a competitive ratio $3+4\sqrt{p(1-p)}$.
summary: We consider search by mobile agents for a hidden, idle target, placed on the infinite line. Feasible solutions are agent trajectories in which all agents reach the target sooner or later. A special feature of our problem is that the agents are $p$-faulty, meaning that every attempt to change direction is an independent Bernoulli trial with known probability $p$, where $p$ is the probability that a turn fails. We are looking for agent trajectories that minimize the worst-case expected termination time, relative to competitive analysis. First, we study linear search with one deterministic $p$-faulty agent, i.e., with no access to random oracles, $p\in (0,1/2)$. For this problem, we provide trajectories that leverage the probabilistic faults into an algorithmic advantage. Our strongest result pertains to a search algorithm (deterministic, aside from the adversarial probabilistic faults) which, as $p\to 0$, has optimal performance $4.59112+ε$, up to the additive term $ε$ that can be arbitrarily small. Additionally, it has performance less than $9$ for $p\leq 0.390388$. When $p\to 1/2$, our algorithm has performance $Θ(1/(1-2p))$, which we also show is optimal up to a constant factor. Second, we consider linear search with two $p$-faulty agents, $p\in (0,1/2)$, for which we provide three algorithms of different advantages, all with a bounded competitive ratio even as $p\rightarrow 1/2$. Indeed, for this problem, we show how the agents can simulate the trajectory of any $0$-faulty agent (deterministic or randomized), independently of the underlying communication model. As a result, searching with two agents allows for a solution with a competitive ratio of $9+ε$, or a competitive ratio of $4.59112+ε$. Our final contribution is a novel algorithm for searching with two $p$-faulty agents that achieves a competitive ratio $3+4\sqrt{p(1-p)}$.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑通过移动代理搜索放置在无限线上的隐藏的空闲目标。可行的解决方案是所有智能体迟早达到目标的智能体轨迹。我们问题的一个特殊特征是代理存在 $p$ 错误，这意味着每次改变方向的尝试都是一次独立的伯努利试验，概率为 $p$，其中 $p$ 是转弯失败的概率。相对于竞争分析，我们正在寻找能够最小化最坏情况预期终止时间的代理轨迹。首先，我们使用一个确定性的 $p$-错误代理来研究线性搜索，即无法访问随机预言，$p\in (0,1/2)$。对于这个问题，我们提供了利用概率错误转化为算法优势的轨迹。我们最强的结果与搜索算法有关（确定性，除了对抗性概率故障），当 $p\to 0$ 时，其最佳性能为 $4.59112+ε$，直到可任意小的加法项 $ε$。此外，它的性能低于 9 美元（$p\leq 0.390388$）。当 $p\to 1/2$ 时，我们的算法具有 $θ(1/(1-2p))$ 性能，我们还表明在常数因子范围内该算法是最佳的。其次，我们考虑使用两个 $p$-faulty 代理 $p\in (0,1/2)$ 的线性搜索，为此我们提供了三种具有不同优势的算法，所有算法都具有有限的竞争比，甚至为 $p\rightarrow 1/2$。事实上，对于这个问题，我们展示了代理如何独立于底层通信模型来模拟任何 $0$ 故障代理（确定性或随机性）的轨迹。因此，使用两个代理进行搜索可以得到竞争比为 $9+ε$ 或竞争比为 $4.59112+ε$ 的解决方案。我们的最终贡献是一种新颖的算法，用于使用两个 $p$-faulty 代理进行搜索，实现竞争比 $3+4\sqrt{p(1-p)}$。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在无限直线上，具有概率性故障的移动代理如何高效搜索隐藏目标的问题，提出了针对单代理和双代理的不同算法策略。
AI.motivation: 移动代理在搜索过程中可能因方向改变故障而失效，传统方法难以处理此类概率性故障，需设计鲁棒算法以最小化最坏情况下的搜索时间。
AI.method: 针对单代理和双代理场景，分别设计确定性算法与协作策略。利用概率故障特性转化为算法优势，并通过模拟无故障代理轨迹实现通信无关的搜索方案。
AI.result: 单代理算法在p趋近于0时达到4.59112+ε的最优性能，双代理算法实现3+4√[p(1-p)]的竞争比，且支持9+ε或4.59112+ε的低竞争比方案。
AI.conclusion: 双代理协作能有效缓解概率故障影响，通过算法设计可将故障转化为优势，为不可靠环境下的搜索问题提供理论最优解与实用方案。

search_text: Overcoming Probabilistic Faults in Disoriented Linear Search 克服迷失方向的线性搜索中的概率错误 We consider search by mobile agents for a hidden, idle target, placed on the infinite line. Feasible solutions are agent trajectories in which all agents reach the target sooner or later. A special feature of our problem is that the agents are $p$-faulty, meaning that every attempt to change direction is an independent Bernoulli trial with known probability $p$, where $p$ is the probability that a turn fails. We are looking for agent trajectories that minimize the worst-case expected termination time, relative to competitive analysis. First, we study linear search with one deterministic $p$-faulty agent, i.e., with no access to random oracles, $p\in (0,1/2)$. For this problem, we provide trajectories that leverage the probabilistic faults into an algorithmic advantage. Our strongest result pertains to a search algorithm (deterministic, aside from the adversarial probabilistic faults) which, as $p\to 0$, has optimal performance $4.59112+ε$, up to the additive term $ε$ that can be arbitrarily small. Additionally, it has performance less than $9$ for $p\leq 0.390388$. When $p\to 1/2$, our algorithm has performance $Θ(1/(1-2p))$, which we also show is optimal up to a constant factor. Second, we consider linear search with two $p$-faulty agents, $p\in (0,1/2)$, for which we provide three algorithms of different advantages, all with a bounded competitive ratio even as $p\rightarrow 1/2$. Indeed, for this problem, we show how the agents can simulate the trajectory of any $0$-faulty agent (deterministic or randomized), independently of the underlying communication model. As a result, searching with two agents allows for a solution with a competitive ratio of $9+ε$, or a competitive ratio of $4.59112+ε$. Our final contribution is a novel algorithm for searching with two $p$-faulty agents that achieves a competitive ratio $3+4\sqrt{p(1-p)}$. 我们考虑通过移动代理搜索放置在无限线上的隐藏的空闲目标。可行的解决方案是所有智能体迟早达到目标的智能体轨迹。我们问题的一个特殊特征是代理存在 $p$ 错误，这意味着每次改变方向的尝试都是一次独立的伯努利试验，概率为 $p$，其中 $p$ 是转弯失败的概率。相对于竞争分析，我们正在寻找能够最小化最坏情况预期终止时间的代理轨迹。首先，我们使用一个确定性的 $p$-错误代理来研究线性搜索，即无法访问随机预言，$p\in (0,1/2)$。对于这个问题，我们提供了利用概率错误转化为算法优势的轨迹。我们最强的结果与搜索算法有关（确定性，除了对抗性概率故障），当 $p\to 0$ 时，其最佳性能为 $4.59112+ε$，直到可任意小的加法项 $ε$。此外，它的性能低于 9 美元（$p\leq 0.390388$）。当 $p\to 1/2$ 时，我们的算法具有 $θ(1/(1-2p))$ 性能，我们还表明在常数因子范围内该算法是最佳的。其次，我们考虑使用两个 $p$-faulty 代理 $p\in (0,1/2)$ 的线性搜索，为此我们提供了三种具有不同优势的算法，所有算法都具有有限的竞争比，甚至为 $p\rightarrow 1/2$。事实上，对于这个问题，我们展示了代理如何独立于底层通信模型来模拟任何 $0$ 故障代理（确定性或随机性）的轨迹。因此，使用两个代理进行搜索可以得到竞争比为 $9+ε$ 或竞争比为 $4.59112+ε$ 的解决方案。我们的最终贡献是一种新颖的算法，用于使用两个 $p$-faulty 代理进行搜索，实现竞争比 $3+4\sqrt{p(1-p)}$。 本文研究了在无限直线上，具有概率性故障的移动代理如何高效搜索隐藏目标的问题，提出了针对单代理和双代理的不同算法策略。 Konstantinos Georgiou,Nikos Giachoudis,Evangelos Kranakis cs.DS,cs.DM

authors_text: Srinath Tankasala,Mitch Pryor
title: Accelerating Trajectory Generation for Quadrotors Using Transformers
title_zh: 使用 Transformer 加速四旋翼飞行器的轨迹生成
abstract: In this work, we address the problem of computation time for trajectory generation in quadrotors. Most trajectory generation methods for waypoint navigation of quadrotors, for example minimum snap/jerk and minimum-time, are structured as bi-level optimizations. The first level involves allocating time across all input waypoints and the second step is to minimize the snap/jerk of the trajectory under that time allocation. Such an optimization can be computationally expensive to solve. In our approach we treat trajectory generation as a supervised learning problem between a sequential set of inputs and outputs. We adapt a transformer model to learn the optimal time allocations for a given set of input waypoints, thus making it into a single step optimization. We demonstrate the performance of the transformer model by training it to predict the time allocations for a minimum snap trajectory generator. The trained transformer model is able to predict accurate time allocations with fewer data samples and smaller model size, compared to a feedforward network (FFN), demonstrating that it is able to model the sequential nature of the waypoint navigation problem.
summary: In this work, we address the problem of computation time for trajectory generation in quadrotors. Most trajectory generation methods for waypoint navigation of quadrotors, for example minimum snap/jerk and minimum-time, are structured as bi-level optimizations. The first level involves allocating time across all input waypoints and the second step is to minimize the snap/jerk of the trajectory under that time allocation. Such an optimization can be computationally expensive to solve. In our approach we treat trajectory generation as a supervised learning problem between a sequential set of inputs and outputs. We adapt a transformer model to learn the optimal time allocations for a given set of input waypoints, thus making it into a single step optimization. We demonstrate the performance of the transformer model by training it to predict the time allocations for a minimum snap trajectory generator. The trained transformer model is able to predict accurate time allocations with fewer data samples and smaller model size, compared to a feedforward network (FFN), demonstrating that it is able to model the sequential nature of the waypoint navigation problem.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们解决了四旋翼飞行器轨迹生成的计算时间问题。用于四旋翼飞行器航路点导航的大多数轨迹生成方法，例如最小急速/急动度和最小时间，都被构造为双层优化。第一级涉及在所有输入航路点之间分配时间，第二步是最小化该时间分配下轨迹的突然/急动。解决这种优化的计算成本可能很高。在我们的方法中，我们将轨迹生成视为一组连续的输入和输出之间的监督学习问题。我们采用变压器模型来学习给定一组输入路点的最佳时间分配，从而使其成为单步优化。我们通过训练 Transformer 模型来预测最小捕捉轨迹生成器的时间分配来展示 Transformer 模型的性能。与前馈网络 (FFN) 相比，经过训练的 Transformer 模型能够使用更少的数据样本和更小的模型大小来预测准确的时间分配，这表明它能够对路点导航问题的顺序性质进行建模。

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用Transformer模型加速四旋翼无人机轨迹生成，通过单步优化替代传统双层优化方法。
AI.motivation: 传统轨迹生成方法（如最小加加速度/加速度）采用双层优化结构，计算成本高且耗时，需寻找更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 将轨迹生成建模为监督学习问题，利用Transformer模型学习输入路点序列的最优时间分配，实现单步优化预测。
AI.result: 训练后的Transformer模型在更少数据样本和更小模型规模下，能准确预测最小加加速度轨迹的时间分配，优于全连接网络。
AI.conclusion: Transformer模型能有效捕捉航点导航的序列特性，为四旋翼无人机轨迹生成提供高效计算框架。

authors_text: Xiang Li,Mingfu Shao
title: On de novo Bridging Paired-end RNA-seq Data
title_zh: 从头桥接配对末端 RNA-seq 数据
abstract: The high-throughput short-reads RNA-seq protocols often produce paired-end reads, with the middle portion of the fragments being unsequenced. We explore if the full-length fragments can be computationally reconstructed from the sequenced two ends in the absence of the reference genome - a problem here we refer to as de novo bridging. Solving this problem provides longer, more informative RNA-seq reads, and benefits downstream RNA-seq analysis such as transcript assembly, expression quantification, and splicing differential analysis. However, de novo bridging is a challenging and complicated task owing to alternative splicing, transcript noises, and sequencing errors. It remains unclear if the data provides sufficient information for accurate bridging, let alone efficient algorithms that determine the true bridges. Methods have been proposed to bridge paired-end reads in the presence of reference genome (called reference-based bridging), but the algorithms are far away from scaling for de novo bridging as the underlying compacted de Bruijn graph(cdBG) used in the latter task often contains millions of vertices and edges. We designed a new truncated Dijkstra's algorithm for this problem, and proposed a novel algorithm that reuses the shortest path tree to avoid running the truncated Dijkstra's algorithm from scratch for all vertices for further speeding up. These innovative techniques result in scalable algorithms that can bridge all paired-end reads in a cdBG with millions of vertices. Our experiments showed that paired-end RNA-seq reads can be accurately bridged to a large extent. The resulting tool is freely available at https://github.com/Shao-Group/rnabridge-denovo.
summary: The high-throughput short-reads RNA-seq protocols often produce paired-end reads, with the middle portion of the fragments being unsequenced. We explore if the full-length fragments can be computationally reconstructed from the sequenced two ends in the absence of the reference genome - a problem here we refer to as de novo bridging. Solving this problem provides longer, more informative RNA-seq reads, and benefits downstream RNA-seq analysis such as transcript assembly, expression quantification, and splicing differential analysis. However, de novo bridging is a challenging and complicated task owing to alternative splicing, transcript noises, and sequencing errors. It remains unclear if the data provides sufficient information for accurate bridging, let alone efficient algorithms that determine the true bridges. Methods have been proposed to bridge paired-end reads in the presence of reference genome (called reference-based bridging), but the algorithms are far away from scaling for de novo bridging as the underlying compacted de Bruijn graph(cdBG) used in the latter task often contains millions of vertices and edges. We designed a new truncated Dijkstra's algorithm for this problem, and proposed a novel algorithm that reuses the shortest path tree to avoid running the truncated Dijkstra's algorithm from scratch for all vertices for further speeding up. These innovative techniques result in scalable algorithms that can bridge all paired-end reads in a cdBG with millions of vertices. Our experiments showed that paired-end RNA-seq reads can be accurately bridged to a large extent. The resulting tool is freely available at https://github.com/Shao-Group/rnabridge-denovo.
abstract_zh: 高通量短读长 RNA-seq 协议通常会产生双端读长，其中片段的中间部分未测序。我们探索在没有参考基因组的情况下是否可以从测序的两端通过计算重建全长片段——这里我们将这个问题称为从头桥接。解决这个问题可以提供更长、信息更丰富的 RNA-seq 读数，并有利于下游 RNA-seq 分析，例如转录本组装、表达定量和剪接差异分析。然而，由于选择性剪接、转录噪音和测序错误，从头桥接是一项具有挑战性和复杂的任务。目前尚不清楚数据是否为准确桥接提供了足够的信息，更不用说确定真正桥接的有效算法了。人们已经提出了在参考基因组存在的情况下桥接双端读取的方法（称为基于参考的桥接），但这些算法距离从头桥接的扩展还很远，因为后者任务中使用的底层压缩 de Bruijn 图（cdBG）通常包含数百万个顶点和边。我们针对这个问题设计了一种新的截断迪杰斯特拉算法，并提出了一种重用最短路径树的新算法，以避免对所有顶点从头开始运行截断迪杰斯特拉算法，从而进一步加速。这些创新技术产生了可扩展的算法，可以桥接具有数百万个顶点的 cdBG 中的所有双端读取。我们的实验表明，双端 RNA-seq 读段可以在很大程度上准确地桥接。生成的工具可在 https://github.com/Shao-Group/rnabridge-denovo 免费获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需参考基因组的从头（de novo）桥接方法，用于重建配对末端RNA-seq数据的全长转录本片段。
AI.motivation: 现有基于参考基因组的桥接方法无法扩展到从头桥接任务，而RNA-seq数据中未测序的中间片段信息对下游分析至关重要，但缺乏高效算法解决从头桥接的挑战。
AI.method: 设计了一种截断Dijkstra算法，并通过复用最短路径树避免重复计算，从而在包含数百万顶点的压缩de Bruijn图中实现可扩展的从头桥接。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能有效准确地桥接大部分配对末端RNA-seq读段，相关工具已开源。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法和工具为无需参考基因组的RNA-seq数据全长重建提供了高效解决方案，提升了转录组分析的准确性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需参考基因组的从头（de novo）桥接方法，用于重建配对末端RNA-seq数据的全长转录本片段。
ai_content.motivation: 现有基于参考基因组的桥接方法无法扩展到从头桥接任务，而RNA-seq数据中未测序的中间片段信息对下游分析至关重要，但缺乏高效算法解决从头桥接的挑战。
ai_content.method: 设计了一种截断Dijkstra算法，并通过复用最短路径树避免重复计算，从而在包含数百万顶点的压缩de Bruijn图中实现可扩展的从头桥接。
ai_content.result: 实验表明，该方法能有效准确地桥接大部分配对末端RNA-seq读段，相关工具已开源。
ai_content.conclusion: 提出的算法和工具为无需参考基因组的RNA-seq数据全长重建提供了高效解决方案，提升了转录组分析的准确性。

search_text: On de novo Bridging Paired-end RNA-seq Data 从头桥接配对末端 RNA-seq 数据 The high-throughput short-reads RNA-seq protocols often produce paired-end reads, with the middle portion of the fragments being unsequenced. We explore if the full-length fragments can be computationally reconstructed from the sequenced two ends in the absence of the reference genome - a problem here we refer to as de novo bridging. Solving this problem provides longer, more informative RNA-seq reads, and benefits downstream RNA-seq analysis such as transcript assembly, expression quantification, and splicing differential analysis. However, de novo bridging is a challenging and complicated task owing to alternative splicing, transcript noises, and sequencing errors. It remains unclear if the data provides sufficient information for accurate bridging, let alone efficient algorithms that determine the true bridges. Methods have been proposed to bridge paired-end reads in the presence of reference genome (called reference-based bridging), but the algorithms are far away from scaling for de novo bridging as the underlying compacted de Bruijn graph(cdBG) used in the latter task often contains millions of vertices and edges. We designed a new truncated Dijkstra's algorithm for this problem, and proposed a novel algorithm that reuses the shortest path tree to avoid running the truncated Dijkstra's algorithm from scratch for all vertices for further speeding up. These innovative techniques result in scalable algorithms that can bridge all paired-end reads in a cdBG with millions of vertices. Our experiments showed that paired-end RNA-seq reads can be accurately bridged to a large extent. The resulting tool is freely available at https://github.com/Shao-Group/rnabridge-denovo. 高通量短读长 RNA-seq 协议通常会产生双端读长，其中片段的中间部分未测序。我们探索在没有参考基因组的情况下是否可以从测序的两端通过计算重建全长片段——这里我们将这个问题称为从头桥接。解决这个问题可以提供更长、信息更丰富的 RNA-seq 读数，并有利于下游 RNA-seq 分析，例如转录本组装、表达定量和剪接差异分析。然而，由于选择性剪接、转录噪音和测序错误，从头桥接是一项具有挑战性和复杂的任务。目前尚不清楚数据是否为准确桥接提供了足够的信息，更不用说确定真正桥接的有效算法了。人们已经提出了在参考基因组存在的情况下桥接双端读取的方法（称为基于参考的桥接），但这些算法距离从头桥接的扩展还很远，因为后者任务中使用的底层压缩 de Bruijn 图（cdBG）通常包含数百万个顶点和边。我们针对这个问题设计了一种新的截断迪杰斯特拉算法，并提出了一种重用最短路径树的新算法，以避免对所有顶点从头开始运行截断迪杰斯特拉算法，从而进一步加速。这些创新技术产生了可扩展的算法，可以桥接具有数百万个顶点的 cdBG 中的所有双端读取。我们的实验表明，双端 RNA-seq 读段可以在很大程度上准确地桥接。生成的工具可在 https://github.com/Shao-Group/rnabridge-denovo 免费获取。 本文提出了一种无需参考基因组的从头（de novo）桥接方法，用于重建配对末端RNA-seq数据的全长转录本片段。 Xiang Li,Mingfu Shao q-bio.GN,cs.DS

authors_text: Rajshekhar Das,Yonatan Dukler,Avinash Ravichandran,Ashwin Swaminathan
title: Learning Expressive Prompting With Residuals for Vision Transformers
title_zh: 使用视觉变压器的残差学习表达提示
abstract: Prompt learning is an efficient approach to adapt transformers by inserting learnable set of parameters into the input and intermediate representations of a pre-trained model. In this work, we present Expressive Prompts with Residuals (EXPRES) which modifies the prompt learning paradigm specifically for effective adaptation of vision transformers (ViT). Out method constructs downstream representations via learnable ``output'' tokens, that are akin to the learned class tokens of the ViT. Further for better steering of the downstream representation processed by the frozen transformer, we introduce residual learnable tokens that are added to the output of various computations. We apply EXPRES for image classification, few shot learning, and semantic segmentation, and show our method is capable of achieving state of the art prompt tuning on 3/3 categories of the VTAB benchmark. In addition to strong performance, we observe that our approach is an order of magnitude more prompt efficient than existing visual prompting baselines. We analytically show the computational benefits of our approach over weight space adaptation techniques like finetuning. Lastly we systematically corroborate the architectural design of our method via a series of ablation experiments.
summary: Prompt learning is an efficient approach to adapt transformers by inserting learnable set of parameters into the input and intermediate representations of a pre-trained model. In this work, we present Expressive Prompts with Residuals (EXPRES) which modifies the prompt learning paradigm specifically for effective adaptation of vision transformers (ViT). Out method constructs downstream representations via learnable ``output'' tokens, that are akin to the learned class tokens of the ViT. Further for better steering of the downstream representation processed by the frozen transformer, we introduce residual learnable tokens that are added to the output of various computations. We apply EXPRES for image classification, few shot learning, and semantic segmentation, and show our method is capable of achieving state of the art prompt tuning on 3/3 categories of the VTAB benchmark. In addition to strong performance, we observe that our approach is an order of magnitude more prompt efficient than existing visual prompting baselines. We analytically show the computational benefits of our approach over weight space adaptation techniques like finetuning. Lastly we systematically corroborate the architectural design of our method via a series of ablation experiments.
abstract_zh: 即时学习是一种通过将可学习的参数集插入到预训练模型的输入和中间表示中来适应 Transformer 的有效方法。在这项工作中，我们提出了带有残差的表达提示（EXPRES），它修改了提示学习范式，专门用于视觉变换器（ViT）的有效适应。 Out 方法通过可学习的“输出”标记构建下游表示，这些标记类似于 ViT 的学习类标记。此外，为了更好地控制冻结变压器处理的下游表示，我们引入了剩余的可学习标记，这些标记被添加到各种计算的输出中。我们将 EXPRES 应用于图像分类、少量镜头学习和语义分割，并表明我们的方法能够在 VTAB 基准的 3/3 类别上实现最先进的提示调整。除了强大的性能之外，我们还观察到我们的方法比现有的视觉提示基线效率更高一个数量级。我们分析地展示了我们的方法相对于微调等权重空间适应技术的计算优势。最后，我们通过一系列消融实验系统地证实了我们方法的架构设计。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EXPRES方法，通过可学习的输出标记和残差标记优化视觉Transformer的提示学习，提升模型适应性与效率。
AI.motivation: 传统提示学习在视觉Transformer上的适应性不足，需更高效且通用的方法以提升下游任务性能并减少参数量。
AI.method: 设计可学习的输出标记（类似ViT的class token）与残差标记，通过残差连接增强对冻结Transformer的控制，应用于分类、少样本学习及分割任务。
AI.result: 在VTAB基准测试中，EXPRES在3/3任务上达到SOTA，提示效率较基线提升10倍，并通过消融实验证明设计有效性。
AI.conclusion: EXPRES在保持高性能的同时显著提升提示效率，为视觉Transformer的适应性学习提供了计算更优的解决方案。

authors_text: Sofia Yfantidou,Marios Constantinides,Dimitris Spathis,Athena Vakali,Daniele Quercia,Fahim Kawsar
title: (Un)fair devices: Moving beyond AI accuracy in personal sensing
title_zh: （不）公平的设备：超越个人感知中的人工智能准确性
abstract: Personal devices are omnipresent in our lives, seamlessly monitoring our activities, from smart rings tracking sleep patterns to smartwatches keeping an eye on missed heartbeats. The rich data streams from such devices fuel advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications. Instead of solely relying on direct sensor measurements, these applications are increasingly leveraging Machine Learning (ML) model estimates to derive insights. But are these estimates biased or not? This literature review delivers compelling evidence about the impact of hidden biases that creep into ML models deployed on personal devices. We discuss critical bias issues drawn from prior work such as racial bias in pulse oximeters, weight bias in optical heart rate sensors, and sex bias in audio-based diagnostics. In response to these challenges, we advocate for a shift from prioritizing performance-oriented evaluations of personal devices to adopting assessments grounded in a human-centered approach. To facilitate this transition, we provide guidelines for the design, development, evaluation, and use of unbiased AI in personal devices, recognizing their potential impact on improving our health, lifestyle, and productivity -- more than any other technology.
summary: Personal devices are omnipresent in our lives, seamlessly monitoring our activities, from smart rings tracking sleep patterns to smartwatches keeping an eye on missed heartbeats. The rich data streams from such devices fuel advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI) applications. Instead of solely relying on direct sensor measurements, these applications are increasingly leveraging Machine Learning (ML) model estimates to derive insights. But are these estimates biased or not? This literature review delivers compelling evidence about the impact of hidden biases that creep into ML models deployed on personal devices. We discuss critical bias issues drawn from prior work such as racial bias in pulse oximeters, weight bias in optical heart rate sensors, and sex bias in audio-based diagnostics. In response to these challenges, we advocate for a shift from prioritizing performance-oriented evaluations of personal devices to adopting assessments grounded in a human-centered approach. To facilitate this transition, we provide guidelines for the design, development, evaluation, and use of unbiased AI in personal devices, recognizing their potential impact on improving our health, lifestyle, and productivity -- more than any other technology.
abstract_zh: 个人设备在我们的生活中无处不在，无缝监控我们的活动，从跟踪睡眠模式的智能戒指到关注错过的心跳的智能手表。来自此类设备的丰富数据流推动了先进的人工智能 (AI) 应用。这些应用程序不再仅仅依赖直接传感器测量，而是越来越多地利用机器学习 (ML) 模型估计来获取见解。但这些估计是否有偏差？这篇文献综述提供了令人信服的证据，证明了个人设备上部署的 ML 模型中隐藏的偏见的影响。我们讨论了先前工作中的关键偏见问题，例如脉搏血氧计中的种族偏见、光学心率传感器中的体重偏见以及基于音频的诊断中的性别偏见。为了应对这些挑战，我们主张从优先考虑以性能为导向的个人设备评估转向采用基于以人为本的方法的评估。为了促进这一转变，我们为个人设备中公正的人工智能的设计、开发、评估和使用提供了指导方针，认识到它们对改善我们的健康、生活方式和生产力的潜在影响——比任何其他技术都更重要。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了个人传感设备中AI模型的隐藏偏见问题，并提出以人类为中心的评估方法以减少偏差。
AI.motivation: 当前个人设备依赖的机器学习模型可能存在种族、性别等偏见，导致健康监测等应用中的不公平结果，需重新审视评估标准。
AI.method: 通过文献回顾分析现有研究中揭示的设备偏见案例（如脉搏血氧仪的种族偏见），并提出无偏见AI设计指南。
AI.result: 证明了ML模型在个人设备中的偏见问题，并提供了从性能导向转向以人为本的评估框架与实施建议。
AI.conclusion: 需通过人类中心方法重构个人传感设备的AI评估体系，以确保技术对健康、生活方式的积极影响。

Instead of solely relying on direct sensor measurements, these applications are increasingly leveraging Machine Learning (ML) model estimates to derive insights. But are these estimates biased or not? This literature review delivers compelling evidence about the impact of hidden biases that creep into ML models deployed on personal devices. We discuss critical bias issues drawn from prior work such as racial bias in pulse oximeters, weight bias in optical heart rate sensors, and sex bias in audio-based diagnostics. In response to these challenges, we advocate for a shift from prioritizing performance-oriented evaluations of personal devices to adopting assessments grounded in a human-centered approach. To facilitate this transition, we provide guidelines for the design, development, evaluation, and use of unbiased AI in personal devices, recognizing their potential impact on improving our health, lifestyle, and productivity -- more than any other technology. 个人设备在我们的生活中无处不在，无缝监控我们的活动，从跟踪睡眠模式的智能戒指到关注错过的心跳的智能手表。来自此类设备的丰富数据流推动了先进的人工智能 (AI) 应用。这些应用程序不再仅仅依赖直接传感器测量，而是越来越多地利用机器学习 (ML) 模型估计来获取见解。但这些估计是否有偏差？这篇文献综述提供了令人信服的证据，证明了个人设备上部署的 ML 模型中隐藏的偏见的影响。我们讨论了先前工作中的关键偏见问题，例如脉搏血氧计中的种族偏见、光学心率传感器中的体重偏见以及基于音频的诊断中的性别偏见。为了应对这些挑战，我们主张从优先考虑以性能为导向的个人设备评估转向采用基于以人为本的方法的评估。为了促进这一转变，我们为个人设备中公正的人工智能的设计、开发、评估和使用提供了指导方针，认识到它们对改善我们的健康、生活方式和生产力的潜在影响——比任何其他技术都更重要。 本文综述了个人传感设备中AI模型的隐藏偏见问题，并提出以人类为中心的评估方法以减少偏差。 Sofia Yfantidou,Marios Constantinides,Dimitris Spathis,Athena Vakali,Daniele Quercia,Fahim Kawsar cs.CY,cs.HC,cs.LG

authors_text: Chin Tseng,Ghulam Jilani Quadri,Zeyu Wang,Danielle Albers Szafir
title: Measuring Categorical Perception in Color-Coded Scatterplots
title_zh: 测量颜色编码散点图中的分类感知
comment: The paper has been accepted to the ACM CHI 2023. 14 pages, 7 figures
comments: The paper has been accepted to the ACM CHI 2023. 14 pages, 7 figures
abstract: Scatterplots commonly use color to encode categorical data. However, as datasets increase in size and complexity, the efficacy of these channels may vary. Designers lack insight into how robust different design choices are to variations in category numbers. This paper presents a crowdsourced experiment measuring how the number of categories and choice of color encodings used in multiclass scatterplots influences the viewers' abilities to analyze data across classes. Participants estimated relative means in a series of scatterplots with 2 to 10 categories encoded using ten color palettes drawn from popular design tools. Our results show that the number of categories and color discriminability within a color palette notably impact people's perception of categorical data in scatterplots and that the judgments become harder as the number of categories grows. We examine existing palette design heuristics in light of our results to help designers make robust color choices informed by the parameters of their data.
summary: Scatterplots commonly use color to encode categorical data. However, as datasets increase in size and complexity, the efficacy of these channels may vary. Designers lack insight into how robust different design choices are to variations in category numbers. This paper presents a crowdsourced experiment measuring how the number of categories and choice of color encodings used in multiclass scatterplots influences the viewers' abilities to analyze data across classes. Participants estimated relative means in a series of scatterplots with 2 to 10 categories encoded using ten color palettes drawn from popular design tools. Our results show that the number of categories and color discriminability within a color palette notably impact people's perception of categorical data in scatterplots and that the judgments become harder as the number of categories grows. We examine existing palette design heuristics in light of our results to help designers make robust color choices informed by the parameters of their data.
abstract_zh: 散点图通常使用颜色来编码分类数据。然而，随着数据集大小和复杂性的增加，这些通道的功效可能会有所不同。设计师缺乏对不同设计选择对类别数量变化的鲁棒性的洞察力。本文提出了一项众包实验，测量多类散点图中使用的类别数量和颜色编码的选择如何影响观看者跨类别分析数据的能力。参与者估计了一系列散点图中的相对平均值，这些散点图中有 2 到 10 个类别，这些类别使用​​从流行设计工具中提取的 10 个调色板进行编码。我们的结果表明，调色板中的类别数量和颜色可辨别性显着影响人们对散点图中分类数据的感知，并且随着类别数量的增加，判断变得更加困难。我们根据我们的结果检查现有的调色板设计启发法，以帮助设计师根据其数据参数做出稳健的颜色选择。

AI.tldr: 本文通过实验研究颜色编码在多类别散点图中对类别感知的影响，分析类别数量和调色板选择如何影响观众的数据分析能力。
AI.motivation: 随着数据集规模和复杂度增加，散点图中颜色编码的效能可能下降，但设计师缺乏关于不同设计选择在类别数量变化下的鲁棒性的洞察。
AI.method: 通过众包实验，让参与者在2至10个类别的散点图中估计相对均值，使用来自流行设计工具的十种调色板进行测试。
AI.result: 类别数量增加和调色板区分度降低显著影响感知效果，判断难度随类别数量增长而提升，现有调色板设计准则需结合数据参数调整。
AI.conclusion: 设计师应根据数据类别数量和颜色区分度选择调色板，以提升散点图中类别数据的可感知性。

authors_text: Fares Bougourzi,Cosimo Distante,Fadi Dornaika,Abdelmalik Taleb-Ahmed
title: D-TrAttUnet: Dual-Decoder Transformer-Based Attention Unet Architecture for Binary and Multi-classes Covid-19 Infection Segmentation
title_zh: D-TrAttUnet：基于双解码器 Transformer 的注意力 Unet 架构，用于二进制和多类 Covid-19 感染分割
abstract: In the last three years, the world has been facing a global crisis caused by Covid-19 pandemic. Medical imaging has been playing a crucial role in the fighting against this disease and saving the human lives. Indeed, CT-scans has proved their efficiency in diagnosing, detecting, and following-up the Covid-19 infection. In this paper, we propose a new Transformer-CNN based approach for Covid-19 infection segmentation from the CT slices. The proposed D-TrAttUnet architecture has an Encoder-Decoder structure, where compound Transformer-CNN encoder and Dual-Decoders are proposed. The Transformer-CNN encoder is built using Transformer layers, UpResBlocks, ResBlocks and max-pooling layers. The Dual-Decoder consists of two identical CNN decoders with attention gates. The two decoders are used to segment the infection and the lung regions simultaneously and the losses of the two tasks are joined. The proposed D-TrAttUnet architecture is evaluated for both Binary and Multi-classes Covid-19 infection segmentation. The experimental results prove the efficiency of the proposed approach to deal with the complexity of Covid-19 segmentation task from limited data. Furthermore, D-TrAttUnet architecture outperforms three baseline CNN segmentation architectures (Unet, AttUnet and Unet++) and three state-of-the-art architectures (AnamNet, SCOATNet and CopleNet), in both Binary and Mutli-classes segmentation tasks.
summary: In the last three years, the world has been facing a global crisis caused by Covid-19 pandemic. Medical imaging has been playing a crucial role in the fighting against this disease and saving the human lives. Indeed, CT-scans has proved their efficiency in diagnosing, detecting, and following-up the Covid-19 infection. In this paper, we propose a new Transformer-CNN based approach for Covid-19 infection segmentation from the CT slices. The proposed D-TrAttUnet architecture has an Encoder-Decoder structure, where compound Transformer-CNN encoder and Dual-Decoders are proposed. The Transformer-CNN encoder is built using Transformer layers, UpResBlocks, ResBlocks and max-pooling layers. The Dual-Decoder consists of two identical CNN decoders with attention gates. The two decoders are used to segment the infection and the lung regions simultaneously and the losses of the two tasks are joined. The proposed D-TrAttUnet architecture is evaluated for both Binary and Multi-classes Covid-19 infection segmentation. The experimental results prove the efficiency of the proposed approach to deal with the complexity of Covid-19 segmentation task from limited data. Furthermore, D-TrAttUnet architecture outperforms three baseline CNN segmentation architectures (Unet, AttUnet and Unet++) and three state-of-the-art architectures (AnamNet, SCOATNet and CopleNet), in both Binary and Mutli-classes segmentation tasks.
abstract_zh: 过去三年来，世界一直面临着由 Covid-19 大流行引发的全球危机。医学成像在对抗这种疾病和拯救人类生命方面发挥着至关重要的作用。事实上，CT 扫描已证明其在诊断、检测和跟踪 Covid-19 感染方面的效率。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 Transformer-CNN 的新方法，用于从 CT 切片中分割 Covid-19 感染。所提出的 D-TrAttUnet 架构具有编码器-解码器结构，其中提出了复合 Transformer-CNN 编码器和双解码器。 Transformer-CNN 编码器是使用 Transformer 层、UpResBlocks、ResBlocks 和最大池层构建的。 Dual-Decoder 由两个相同的带有注意力门的 CNN 解码器组成。两个解码器用于同时分割感染和肺部区域，并将两个任务的损失相加。所提出的 D-TrAttUnet 架构针对二元和多类 Covid-19 感染分割进行了评估。实验结果证明了所提出的方法在处理有限数据的 Covid-19 分割任务的复杂性方面的效率。此外，D-TrAttUnet 架构在二元和多类分割任务中均优于三种基线 CNN 分割架构（Unet、AttUnet 和 Unet++）和三种最先进的架构（AnamNet、SCOATNet 和 CopleNet）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer-CNN的D-TrAttUnet架构，用于从CT切片中进行二分类和多分类的Covid-19感染分割。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情需要高效准确的医学影像分析工具，CT扫描在诊断中至关重要，但感染分割任务因数据有限和病变复杂性而具有挑战性。
AI.method: 设计包含Transformer-CNN编码器和双解码器的架构，编码器融合Transformer层与残差模块，双解码器通过注意力门机制同步分割感染区域与肺部区域，并联合优化两任务损失。
AI.result: 在有限数据下，D-TrAttUnet在二分类和多分类任务中均优于Unet、AttUnet等经典模型及AnamNet等先进模型，分割精度显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 双解码器与Transformer-CNN的结合有效解决了Covid-19分割的复杂性问题，为医学影像分析提供了新思路。

authors_text: Ruyi Ding,Cheng Gongye,Siyue Wang,Aidong Ding,Yunsi Fei
title: EMShepherd: Detecting Adversarial Samples via Side-channel Leakage
title_zh: EMShepherd：通过侧通道泄漏检测对抗性样本
abstract: Deep Neural Networks (DNN) are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations-small changes crafted deliberately on the input to mislead the model for wrong predictions. Adversarial attacks have disastrous consequences for deep learning-empowered critical applications. Existing defense and detection techniques both require extensive knowledge of the model, testing inputs, and even execution details. They are not viable for general deep learning implementations where the model internal is unknown, a common 'black-box' scenario for model users. Inspired by the fact that electromagnetic (EM) emanations of a model inference are dependent on both operations and data and may contain footprints of different input classes, we propose a framework, EMShepherd, to capture EM traces of model execution, perform processing on traces and exploit them for adversarial detection. Only benign samples and their EM traces are used to train the adversarial detector: a set of EM classifiers and class-specific unsupervised anomaly detectors. When the victim model system is under attack by an adversarial example, the model execution will be different from executions for the known classes, and the EM trace will be different. We demonstrate that our air-gapped EMShepherd can effectively detect different adversarial attacks on a commonly used FPGA deep learning accelerator for both Fashion MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets. It achieves a 100% detection rate on most types of adversarial samples, which is comparable to the state-of-the-art 'white-box' software-based detectors.
summary: Deep Neural Networks (DNN) are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations-small changes crafted deliberately on the input to mislead the model for wrong predictions. Adversarial attacks have disastrous consequences for deep learning-empowered critical applications. Existing defense and detection techniques both require extensive knowledge of the model, testing inputs, and even execution details. They are not viable for general deep learning implementations where the model internal is unknown, a common 'black-box' scenario for model users. Inspired by the fact that electromagnetic (EM) emanations of a model inference are dependent on both operations and data and may contain footprints of different input classes, we propose a framework, EMShepherd, to capture EM traces of model execution, perform processing on traces and exploit them for adversarial detection. Only benign samples and their EM traces are used to train the adversarial detector: a set of EM classifiers and class-specific unsupervised anomaly detectors. When the victim model system is under attack by an adversarial example, the model execution will be different from executions for the known classes, and the EM trace will be different. We demonstrate that our air-gapped EMShepherd can effectively detect different adversarial attacks on a commonly used FPGA deep learning accelerator for both Fashion MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets. It achieves a 100% detection rate on most types of adversarial samples, which is comparable to the state-of-the-art 'white-box' software-based detectors.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络 (DNN) 很容易受到对抗性扰动的影响，即对输入故意进行的微小更改会误导模型做出错误的预测。对抗性攻击会给深度学习支持的关键应用带来灾难性的后果。现有的防御和检测技术都需要对模型、测试输入甚至执行细节的广泛了解。它们不适用于模型内部未知的一般深度学习实现，这是模型用户常见的“黑匣子”场景。受模型推理的电磁 (EM) 发射依赖于操作和数据并且可能包含不同输入类的足迹这一事实的启发，我们提出了一个框架 EMShepherd，用于捕获模型执行的 EM 痕迹，对痕迹进行处理并利用它们进行对抗性检测。仅使用良性样本及其 EM 轨迹来训练对抗性检测器：一组 EM 分类器和特定于类别的无监督异常检测器。当受害者模型系统受到对抗性示例的攻击时，模型执行将与已知类的执行不同，并且 EM 跟踪也会不同。我们证明，我们的气隙 EMShepherd 可以有效检测针对 Fashion MNIST 和 CIFAR-10 数据集的常用 FPGA 深度学习加速器的不同对抗性攻击。它对大多数类型的对抗样本实现了 100% 的检测率，可与最先进的基于“白盒”软件的检测器相媲美。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EMShepherd框架，通过捕捉模型执行时的电磁信号泄露，检测对抗样本，解决黑盒场景下模型内部信息未知的检测难题。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗样本检测方法依赖模型结构和输入测试信息，在黑盒场景下不可行。电磁信号受模型运算和数据影响，可能包含输入类别特征，可作为检测依据。
AI.method: 采集模型执行时的电磁信号，利用良性样本训练电磁分类器和异常检测器。通过对比正常与攻击场景下的电磁信号差异，识别对抗样本。
AI.result: 在FPGA加速器上针对Fashion MNIST和CIFAR-10数据集的实验显示，EMShepherd对多数对抗样本检测率达100%，性能与白盒软件检测器相当。
AI.conclusion: EMShepherd通过电磁侧信道实现黑盒场景下的高效对抗检测，无需模型内部信息，为实际深度学习系统安全防护提供新思路。

authors_text: Tao Sun,Lu Pang,Weimin Lyu,Chao Chen,Haibin Ling
title: Mask and Restore: Blind Backdoor Defense at Test Time with Masked Autoencoder
title_zh: 屏蔽和恢复：使用屏蔽自动编码器在测试时进行盲后门防御
abstract: Deep neural networks are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where an adversary manipulates the model behavior through overlaying images with special triggers. Existing backdoor defense methods often require accessing a few validation data and model parameters, which is impractical in many real-world applications, e.g., when the model is provided as a cloud service. In this paper, we address the practical task of blind backdoor defense at test time, in particular for local attacks and black-box models. The true label of every test image needs to be recovered on the fly from a suspicious model regardless of image benignity. We consider test-time image purification that incapacitates local triggers while keeping semantic contents intact. Due to diverse trigger patterns and sizes, the heuristic trigger search can be unscalable. We circumvent such barrier by leveraging the strong reconstruction power of generative models, and propose Blind Defense with Masked AutoEncoder (BDMAE). BDMAE detects possible local triggers using image structural similarity and label consistency between the test image and MAE restorations. The detection results are then refined by considering trigger topology. Finally, we fuse MAE restorations adaptively into a purified image for making prediction. Extensive experiments under different backdoor settings validate its effectiveness and generalizability.
summary: Deep neural networks are vulnerable to backdoor attacks, where an adversary manipulates the model behavior through overlaying images with special triggers. Existing backdoor defense methods often require accessing a few validation data and model parameters, which is impractical in many real-world applications, e.g., when the model is provided as a cloud service. In this paper, we address the practical task of blind backdoor defense at test time, in particular for local attacks and black-box models. The true label of every test image needs to be recovered on the fly from a suspicious model regardless of image benignity. We consider test-time image purification that incapacitates local triggers while keeping semantic contents intact. Due to diverse trigger patterns and sizes, the heuristic trigger search can be unscalable. We circumvent such barrier by leveraging the strong reconstruction power of generative models, and propose Blind Defense with Masked AutoEncoder (BDMAE). BDMAE detects possible local triggers using image structural similarity and label consistency between the test image and MAE restorations. The detection results are then refined by considering trigger topology. Finally, we fuse MAE restorations adaptively into a purified image for making prediction. Extensive experiments under different backdoor settings validate its effectiveness and generalizability.
abstract_zh: 深度神经网络很容易受到后门攻击，攻击者通过使用特殊触发器覆盖图像来操纵模型行为。现有的后门防御方法通常需要访问一些验证数据和模型参数，这在许多实际应用中是不切实际的，例如当模型作为云服务提供时。在本文中，我们解决了测试时盲目后门防御的实际任务，特别是针对本地攻击和黑盒模型。无论图像良性如何，每个测试图像的真实标签都需要从可疑模型中即时恢复。我们考虑测试时图像净化，使本地触发器失效，同时保持语义内容完整。由于触发模式和大小不同，启发式触发搜索可能无法扩展。我们通过利用生成模型强大的重建能力来规避这种障碍，并提出了带有掩码自动编码器的盲防御（BDMAE）。 BDMAE 使用测试图像和 MAE 恢复之间的图像结构相似性和标签一致性来检测可能的局部触发。然后通过考虑触发拓扑来细化检测结果。最后，我们将 MAE 恢复自适应地融合到纯化图像中以进行预测。不同后门设置下的大量实验验证了其有效性和普遍性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在测试时无需模型参数和验证数据的盲后门防御方法BDMAE，通过掩码自编码器恢复图像语义内容并消除局部触发器。
AI.motivation: 现有后门防御方法依赖验证数据和模型参数，在云服务等实际场景中难以应用，需开发无需模型访问的测试时盲防御方案。
AI.method: 利用掩码自编码器（MAE）的重建能力，通过图像结构相似性与标签一致性检测触发器，结合触发器拓扑优化检测结果，并自适应融合MAE重建图像生成净化样本。
AI.result: 在多种后门攻击设置下验证了BDMAE的有效性和泛化性，能成功检测并消除局部触发器且保持语义完整性。
AI.conclusion: BDMAE为黑盒模型提供了无需模型访问的实用后门防御方案，通过生成模型的重建能力实现测试时图像净化。

authors_text: Zhipeng Bao,Pavel Tokmakov,Yu-Xiong Wang,Adrien Gaidon,Martial Hebert
title: Object Discovery from Motion-Guided Tokens
title_zh: 从运动引导令牌发现对象
abstract: Object discovery -- separating objects from the background without manual labels -- is a fundamental open challenge in computer vision. Previous methods struggle to go beyond clustering of low-level cues, whether handcrafted (e.g., color, texture) or learned (e.g., from auto-encoders). In this work, we augment the auto-encoder representation learning framework with two key components: motion-guidance and mid-level feature tokenization. Although both have been separately investigated, we introduce a new transformer decoder showing that their benefits can compound thanks to motion-guided vector quantization. We show that our architecture effectively leverages the synergy between motion and tokenization, improving upon the state of the art on both synthetic and real datasets. Our approach enables the emergence of interpretable object-specific mid-level features, demonstrating the benefits of motion-guidance (no labeling) and quantization (interpretability, memory efficiency).
summary: Object discovery -- separating objects from the background without manual labels -- is a fundamental open challenge in computer vision. Previous methods struggle to go beyond clustering of low-level cues, whether handcrafted (e.g., color, texture) or learned (e.g., from auto-encoders). In this work, we augment the auto-encoder representation learning framework with two key components: motion-guidance and mid-level feature tokenization. Although both have been separately investigated, we introduce a new transformer decoder showing that their benefits can compound thanks to motion-guided vector quantization. We show that our architecture effectively leverages the synergy between motion and tokenization, improving upon the state of the art on both synthetic and real datasets. Our approach enables the emergence of interpretable object-specific mid-level features, demonstrating the benefits of motion-guidance (no labeling) and quantization (interpretability, memory efficiency).
abstract_zh: 对象发现——无需手动标签将对象与背景分离——是计算机视觉中的一个基本的开放挑战。以前的方法很难超越低级线索的聚类，无论是手工制作的（例如颜色、纹理）还是学习的（例如从自动编码器）。在这项工作中，我们用两个关键组件增强了自动编码器表示学习框架：运动引导和中级特征标记化。尽管两者都已被单独研究，但我们引入了一种新的转换器解码器，表明由于运动引导矢量量化，它们的好处可以得到复合。我们表明，我们的架构有效地利用了运动和标记化之间的协同作用，改进了合成数据集和真实数据集的最新技术。我们的方法使得可解释的特定于对象的中级特征的出现成为可能，展示了运动引导（无标签）和量化（可解释性、内存效率）的好处。

AI.motivation: 现有方法难以超越低级线索聚类，无法有效分离物体与背景，且缺乏可解释性。
AI.method: 在自编码器框架中引入运动引导机制与中层特征标记化，并设计新型Transformer解码器实现运动引导的向量量化。
AI.result: 在合成与真实数据集上均超越现有方法，生成可解释的物体特异性中层特征，兼具可解释性与内存效率。
AI.conclusion: 运动引导与标记化的协同提升了物体发现性能，为无监督场景分割提供了新思路。

search_text: Object Discovery from Motion-Guided Tokens 从运动引导令牌发现对象 Object discovery -- separating objects from the background without manual labels -- is a fundamental open challenge in computer vision. Previous methods struggle to go beyond clustering of low-level cues, whether handcrafted (e.g., color, texture) or learned (e.g., from auto-encoders). In this work, we augment the auto-encoder representation learning framework with two key components: motion-guidance and mid-level feature tokenization. Although both have been separately investigated, we introduce a new transformer decoder showing that their benefits can compound thanks to motion-guided vector quantization. We show that our architecture effectively leverages the synergy between motion and tokenization, improving upon the state of the art on both synthetic and real datasets. Our approach enables the emergence of interpretable object-specific mid-level features, demonstrating the benefits of motion-guidance (no labeling) and quantization (interpretability, memory efficiency). 对象发现——无需手动标签将对象与背景分离——是计算机视觉中的一个基本的开放挑战。以前的方法很难超越低级线索的聚类，无论是手工制作的（例如颜色、纹理）还是学习的（例如从自动编码器）。在这项工作中，我们用两个关键组件增强了自动编码器表示学习框架：运动引导和中级特征标记化。尽管两者都已被单独研究，但我们引入了一种新的转换器解码器，表明由于运动引导矢量量化，它们的好处可以得到复合。我们表明，我们的架构有效地利用了运动和标记化之间的协同作用，改进了合成数据集和真实数据集的最新技术。我们的方法使得可解释的特定于对象的中级特征的出现成为可能，展示了运动引导（无标签）和量化（可解释性、内存效率）的好处。 本文提出一种无需人工标注即可从运动引导的标记中发现物体的无监督方法。 Zhipeng Bao,Pavel Tokmakov,Yu-Xiong Wang,Adrien Gaidon,Martial Hebert cs.CV

authors_text: Yiqing Shen,Pengfei Guo,Jingpu Wu,Qianqi Huang,Nhat Le,Jinyuan Zhou,Shanshan Jiang,Mathias Unberath
title: MoViT: Memorizing Vision Transformers for Medical Image Analysis
title_zh: MoViT：记住用于医学图像分析的视觉变换器
abstract: The synergy of long-range dependencies from transformers and local representations of image content from convolutional neural networks (CNNs) has led to advanced architectures and increased performance for various medical image analysis tasks due to their complementary benefits. However, compared with CNNs, transformers require considerably more training data, due to a larger number of parameters and an absence of inductive bias. The need for increasingly large datasets continues to be problematic, particularly in the context of medical imaging, where both annotation efforts and data protection result in limited data availability. In this work, inspired by the human decision-making process of correlating new evidence with previously memorized experience, we propose a Memorizing Vision Transformer (MoViT) to alleviate the need for large-scale datasets to successfully train and deploy transformer-based architectures. MoViT leverages an external memory structure to cache history attention snapshots during the training stage. To prevent overfitting, we incorporate an innovative memory update scheme, attention temporal moving average, to update the stored external memories with the historical moving average. For inference speedup, we design a prototypical attention learning method to distill the external memory into smaller representative subsets. We evaluate our method on a public histology image dataset and an in-house MRI dataset, demonstrating that MoViT applied to varied medical image analysis tasks, can outperform vanilla transformer models across varied data regimes, especially in cases where only a small amount of annotated data is available. More importantly, MoViT can reach a competitive performance of ViT with only 3.0% of the training data.
summary: The synergy of long-range dependencies from transformers and local representations of image content from convolutional neural networks (CNNs) has led to advanced architectures and increased performance for various medical image analysis tasks due to their complementary benefits. However, compared with CNNs, transformers require considerably more training data, due to a larger number of parameters and an absence of inductive bias. The need for increasingly large datasets continues to be problematic, particularly in the context of medical imaging, where both annotation efforts and data protection result in limited data availability. In this work, inspired by the human decision-making process of correlating new evidence with previously memorized experience, we propose a Memorizing Vision Transformer (MoViT) to alleviate the need for large-scale datasets to successfully train and deploy transformer-based architectures. MoViT leverages an external memory structure to cache history attention snapshots during the training stage. To prevent overfitting, we incorporate an innovative memory update scheme, attention temporal moving average, to update the stored external memories with the historical moving average. For inference speedup, we design a prototypical attention learning method to distill the external memory into smaller representative subsets. We evaluate our method on a public histology image dataset and an in-house MRI dataset, demonstrating that MoViT applied to varied medical image analysis tasks, can outperform vanilla transformer models across varied data regimes, especially in cases where only a small amount of annotated data is available. More importantly, MoViT can reach a competitive performance of ViT with only 3.0% of the training data.
abstract_zh: 变压器的远程依赖性和卷积神经网络 (CNN) 的图像内容的局部表示的协同作用，由于其互补的优势，带来了先进的架构并提高了各种医学图像分析任务的性能。然而，与 CNN 相比，由于参数数量较多且缺乏归纳偏差，Transformer 需要更多的训练数据。对越来越大的数据集的需求仍然是个问题，特别是在医学成像的背景下，注释工作和数据保护都会导致数据可用性有限。在这项工作中，受到将新证据与先前记忆的经验相关联的人类决策过程的启发，我们提出了一种记忆视觉变换器（MoViT），以减轻对大规模数据集的需求，以成功训练和部署基于变换器的架构。 MoViT 利用外部内存结构在训练阶段缓存历史注意力快照。为了防止过度拟合，我们采用了一种创新的记忆更新方案，即注意力时间移动平均值，以使用历史移动平均值更新存储的外部记忆。为了加速推理，我们设计了一种原型注意力学习方法，将外部记忆提炼成更小的代表性子集。我们在公共组织学图像数据集和内部 MRI 数据集上评估我们的方法，证明 MoViT 应用于各种医学图像分析任务，可以在各种数据体系中优于普通 Transformer 模型，特别是在只有少量注释数据可用的情况下。更重要的是，MoViT 只需 3.0% 的训练数据就可以达到 ViT 的竞争性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为MoViT的记忆视觉Transformer模型，旨在减少医学图像分析中对大规模数据集的依赖。
AI.motivation: 与CNN相比，Transformer需要更多训练数据，而医学图像数据因标注成本高和隐私保护限制难以获取，亟需降低数据需求的模型。
AI.method: 通过引入外部记忆结构存储训练时的注意力快照，结合注意力时间滑动平均更新机制防止过拟合，并设计原型注意力学习方法加速推理。
AI.result: 在公开组织学图像数据集和内部MRI数据集上，MoViT在少量标注数据下优于传统Transformer模型，仅需3%训练数据即可达到ViT的性能。
AI.conclusion: MoViT通过记忆机制有效缓解了医学图像分析中数据稀缺问题，为低数据场景下的Transformer应用提供了可行方案。
