authors_text: Pengwei Liang,Junjun Jiang,Xianming Liu,Jiayi Ma
title: Image Deblurring by Exploring In-depth Properties of Transformer
title_zh: 通过探索 Transformer 的深入特性实现图像去模糊
comment: accept by IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
comments: accept by IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Image deblurring continues to achieve impressive performance with the development of generative models. Nonetheless, there still remains a displeasing problem if one wants to improve perceptual quality and quantitative scores of recovered image at the same time. In this study, drawing inspiration from the research of transformer properties, we introduce the pretrained transformers to address this problem. In particular, we leverage deep features extracted from a pretrained vision transformer (ViT) to encourage recovered images to be sharp without sacrificing the performance measured by the quantitative metrics. The pretrained transformer can capture the global topological relations (i.e., self-similarity) of image, and we observe that the captured topological relations about the sharp image will change when blur occurs. By comparing the transformer features between recovered image and target one, the pretrained transformer provides high-resolution blur-sensitive semantic information, which is critical in measuring the sharpness of the deblurred image. On the basis of the advantages, we present two types of novel perceptual losses to guide image deblurring. One regards the features as vectors and computes the discrepancy between representations extracted from recovered image and target one in Euclidean space. The other type considers the features extracted from an image as a distribution and compares the distribution discrepancy between recovered image and target one. We demonstrate the effectiveness of transformer properties in improving the perceptual quality while not sacrificing the quantitative scores (PSNR) over the most competitive models, such as Uformer, Restormer, and NAFNet, on defocus deblurring and motion deblurring tasks.
summary: Image deblurring continues to achieve impressive performance with the development of generative models. Nonetheless, there still remains a displeasing problem if one wants to improve perceptual quality and quantitative scores of recovered image at the same time. In this study, drawing inspiration from the research of transformer properties, we introduce the pretrained transformers to address this problem. In particular, we leverage deep features extracted from a pretrained vision transformer (ViT) to encourage recovered images to be sharp without sacrificing the performance measured by the quantitative metrics. The pretrained transformer can capture the global topological relations (i.e., self-similarity) of image, and we observe that the captured topological relations about the sharp image will change when blur occurs. By comparing the transformer features between recovered image and target one, the pretrained transformer provides high-resolution blur-sensitive semantic information, which is critical in measuring the sharpness of the deblurred image. On the basis of the advantages, we present two types of novel perceptual losses to guide image deblurring. One regards the features as vectors and computes the discrepancy between representations extracted from recovered image and target one in Euclidean space. The other type considers the features extracted from an image as a distribution and compares the distribution discrepancy between recovered image and target one. We demonstrate the effectiveness of transformer properties in improving the perceptual quality while not sacrificing the quantitative scores (PSNR) over the most competitive models, such as Uformer, Restormer, and NAFNet, on defocus deblurring and motion deblurring tasks.
abstract_zh: 随着生成模型的发展，图像去模糊继续取得令人印象深刻的性能。尽管如此，如果想要同时提高恢复图像的感知质量和定量分数，仍然存在一个令人不满意的问题。在本研究中，从变压器特性研究中汲取灵感，我们引入了预训练变压器来解决这个问题。特别是，我们利用从预训练视觉转换器（ViT）中提取的深层特征来鼓励恢复的图像清晰，而不牺牲定量指标测量的性能。预训练的 Transformer 可以捕获图像的全局拓扑关系（即自相似性），我们观察到捕获的清晰图像的拓扑关系在发生模糊时会发生变化。通过比较恢复图像和目标图像之间的变换器特征，预训练变换器提供高分辨率模糊敏感语义信息，这对于测量去模糊图像的清晰度至关重要。基于这些优点，我们提出了两种新颖的感知损失来指导图像去模糊。我们将这些特征视为向量，并计算从恢复图像中提取的表示与欧几里得空间中的目标图像之间的差异。另一种类型将从图像中提取的特征视为分布，并比较恢复图像和目标图像之间的分布差异。我们在散焦去模糊和运动去模糊任务中证明了 Transformer 属性在提高感知质量方面的有效性，同时不牺牲最具竞争力的模型（例如 Uformer、Restormer 和 NAFNet）的定量分数 (PSNR)。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用预训练Transformer的特性来改进图像去模糊任务，同时提升感知质量和定量指标。
AI.motivation: 现有去模糊方法难以同时提升恢复图像的感知质量（如视觉锐度）和定量指标（如PSNR），需探索新的感知损失函数以平衡两者。
AI.method: 引入预训练视觉Transformer（ViT）提取深层特征，设计两种感知损失：基于欧氏空间向量差异的特征损失和基于分布差异的分布损失，利用Transformer对模糊前后图像拓扑关系变化的敏感性指导去模糊过程。
AI.result: 在去模糊任务中，相比Uformer、Restormer等模型，本文方法在保持PSNR等定量指标的同时显著提升了感知质量，验证了Transformer特性在去模糊中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 通过挖掘Transformer的全局拓扑关系特性，设计的感知损失能够有效平衡图像去模糊的感知质量与定量性能，为后续研究提供新思路。

motivation: 现有去模糊方法难以同时提升恢复图像的感知质量（如视觉锐度）和定量指标（如PSNR），需探索新的感知损失函数以平衡两者。
method: 引入预训练视觉Transformer（ViT）提取深层特征，设计两种感知损失：基于欧氏空间向量差异的特征损失和基于分布差异的分布损失，利用Transformer对模糊前后图像拓扑关系变化的敏感性指导去模糊过程。
result: 在去模糊任务中，相比Uformer、Restormer等模型，本文方法在保持PSNR等定量指标的同时显著提升了感知质量，验证了Transformer特性在去模糊中的有效性。
conclusion: 通过挖掘Transformer的全局拓扑关系特性，设计的感知损失能够有效平衡图像去模糊的感知质量与定量性能，为后续研究提供新思路。
search_text: Image Deblurring by Exploring In-depth Properties of Transformer 通过探索 Transformer 的深入特性实现图像去模糊 Image deblurring continues to achieve impressive performance with the development of generative models. Nonetheless, there still remains a displeasing problem if one wants to improve perceptual quality and quantitative scores of recovered image at the same time. In this study, drawing inspiration from the research of transformer properties, we introduce the pretrained transformers to address this problem. In particular, we leverage deep features extracted from a pretrained vision transformer (ViT) to encourage recovered images to be sharp without sacrificing the performance measured by the quantitative metrics. The pretrained transformer can capture the global topological relations (i.e., self-similarity) of image, and we observe that the captured topological relations about the sharp image will change when blur occurs. By comparing the transformer features between recovered image and target one, the pretrained transformer provides high-resolution blur-sensitive semantic information, which is critical in measuring the sharpness of the deblurred image. On the basis of the advantages, we present two types of novel perceptual losses to guide image deblurring. One regards the features as vectors and computes the discrepancy between representations extracted from recovered image and target one in Euclidean space. The other type considers the features extracted from an image as a distribution and compares the distribution discrepancy between recovered image and target one. We demonstrate the effectiveness of transformer properties in improving the perceptual quality while not sacrificing the quantitative scores (PSNR) over the most competitive models, such as Uformer, Restormer, and NAFNet, on defocus deblurring and motion deblurring tasks. 随着生成模型的发展，图像去模糊继续取得令人印象深刻的性能。尽管如此，如果想要同时提高恢复图像的感知质量和定量分数，仍然存在一个令人不满意的问题。在本研究中，从变压器特性研究中汲取灵感，我们引入了预训练变压器来解决这个问题。特别是，我们利用从预训练视觉转换器（ViT）中提取的深层特征来鼓励恢复的图像清晰，而不牺牲定量指标测量的性能。预训练的 Transformer 可以捕获图像的全局拓扑关系（即自相似性），我们观察到捕获的清晰图像的拓扑关系在发生模糊时会发生变化。通过比较恢复图像和目标图像之间的变换器特征，预训练变换器提供高分辨率模糊敏感语义信息，这对于测量去模糊图像的清晰度至关重要。基于这些优点，我们提出了两种新颖的感知损失来指导图像去模糊。我们将这些特征视为向量，并计算从恢复图像中提取的表示与欧几里得空间中的目标图像之间的差异。另一种类型将从图像中提取的特征视为分布，并比较恢复图像和目标图像之间的分布差异。我们在散焦去模糊和运动去模糊任务中证明了 Transformer 属性在提高感知质量方面的有效性，同时不牺牲最具竞争力的模型（例如 Uformer、Restormer 和 NAFNet）的定量分数 (PSNR)。 本文提出利用预训练Transformer的特性来改进图像去模糊任务，同时提升感知质量和定量指标。 Pengwei Liang,Junjun Jiang,Xianming Liu,Jiayi Ma cs.CV

authors_text: Nayara Fonseca,Veronica Guidetti,Will Trojak
title: Probing optimisation in physics-informed neural networks
title_zh: 物理信息神经网络中的探测优化
comment: Accepted at the ICLR 2023 Workshop on Physics for Machine Learning
comments: Accepted at the ICLR 2023 Workshop on Physics for Machine Learning
abstract: A novel comparison is presented of the effect of optimiser choice on the accuracy of physics-informed neural networks (PINNs). To give insight into why some optimisers are better, a new approach is proposed that tracks the training trajectory curvature and can be evaluated on the fly at a low computational cost. The linear advection equation is studied for several advective velocities, and we show that the optimiser choice substantially impacts PINNs model performance and accuracy. Furthermore, using the curvature measure, we found a negative correlation between the convergence error and the curvature in the optimiser local reference frame. It is concluded that, in this case, larger local curvature values result in better solutions. Consequently, optimisation of PINNs is made more difficult as minima are in highly curved regions.
summary: A novel comparison is presented of the effect of optimiser choice on the accuracy of physics-informed neural networks (PINNs). To give insight into why some optimisers are better, a new approach is proposed that tracks the training trajectory curvature and can be evaluated on the fly at a low computational cost. The linear advection equation is studied for several advective velocities, and we show that the optimiser choice substantially impacts PINNs model performance and accuracy. Furthermore, using the curvature measure, we found a negative correlation between the convergence error and the curvature in the optimiser local reference frame. It is concluded that, in this case, larger local curvature values result in better solutions. Consequently, optimisation of PINNs is made more difficult as minima are in highly curved regions.
abstract_zh: 对优化器选择对物理信息神经网络 (PINN) 准确性的影响进行了新颖的比较。为了深入了解为什么某些优化器更好，提出了一种新方法来跟踪训练轨迹曲率，并可以以较低的计算成本进行动态评估。研究了几种平流速度的线性平流方程，我们表明优化器的选择会极大地影响 PINN 模型的性能和准确性。此外，使用曲率测量，我们发现收敛误差与优化器局部参考系中的曲率之间呈负相关。结论是，在这种情况下，较大的局部曲率值会产生更好的解。因此，PINN 的优化变得更加困难，因为最小值位于高度弯曲的区域。

AI.tldr: 本文比较了不同优化器对物理信息神经网络（PINNs）准确性的影响，并提出了一种跟踪训练轨迹曲率的新方法，以分析优化器选择对模型性能的影响。
AI.motivation: 物理信息神经网络（PINNs）的优化效果受优化器选择影响显著，但缺乏对优化过程曲率特征的系统分析，导致难以指导优化器选择。
AI.method: 提出一种实时追踪训练轨迹曲率的方法，通过计算优化器局部参考系中的曲率，评估其与收敛误差的相关性。
AI.result: 发现优化器选择显著影响PINNs性能，曲率与收敛误差呈负相关，高曲率区域对应更优解，但高度弯曲的极小值区域使优化难度增加。
AI.conclusion: 优化器选择对PINNs性能至关重要，高曲率区域虽能提升精度，但会增加优化难度，需进一步研究曲率与优化策略的关联。

authors_text: Alberto Villani,Giovanni Cortigiani,Bernardo Brogi,Nicole D'Aurizio,Tommaso Lisini Baldi,Domenico Prattichizzo
title: Avatarm: an Avatar With Manipulation Capabilities for the Physical Metaverse
title_zh: Avatarm：具有物理虚拟宇宙操控能力的化身
comment: ef:2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: ef:2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: Metaverse is an immersive shared space that remote users can access through virtual and augmented reality interfaces, enabling their avatars to interact with each other and the surrounding. Although digital objects can be manipulated, physical objects cannot be touched, grasped, or moved within the metaverse due to the lack of a suitable interface. This work proposes a solution to overcome this limitation by introducing the concept of a Physical Metaverse enabled by a new interface named "Avatarm". The Avatarm consists in an avatar enhanced with a robotic arm that performs physical manipulation tasks while remaining entirely hidden in the metaverse. The users have the illusion that the avatar is directly manipulating objects without the mediation by a robot. The Avatarm is the first step towards a new metaverse, the "Physical Metaverse", where users can physically interact each other and with the environment.
summary: Metaverse is an immersive shared space that remote users can access through virtual and augmented reality interfaces, enabling their avatars to interact with each other and the surrounding. Although digital objects can be manipulated, physical objects cannot be touched, grasped, or moved within the metaverse due to the lack of a suitable interface. This work proposes a solution to overcome this limitation by introducing the concept of a Physical Metaverse enabled by a new interface named "Avatarm". The Avatarm consists in an avatar enhanced with a robotic arm that performs physical manipulation tasks while remaining entirely hidden in the metaverse. The users have the illusion that the avatar is directly manipulating objects without the mediation by a robot. The Avatarm is the first step towards a new metaverse, the "Physical Metaverse", where users can physically interact each other and with the environment.
abstract_zh: Metaverse 是一个沉浸式共享空间，远程用户可以通过虚拟和增强现实界面访问该空间，使他们的化身能够与彼此以及周围环境进行交互。尽管可以操纵数字对象，但由于缺乏合适的界面，物理对象无法在虚拟宇宙中触摸、抓握或移动。这项工作提出了一种解决方案，通过引入由名为“Avatarm”的新界面启用的物理元宇宙的概念来克服这一限制。 Avatarm 是一个由机械臂增强的化身，可以执行物理操作任务，同时完全隐藏在虚拟宇宙中。用户有一种错觉，认为化身在没有机器人中介的情况下直接操纵物体。 Avatarm 是迈向新元宇宙（“物理元宇宙”）的第一步，用户可以在其中彼此之间以及与环境进行物理交互。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Avatarm的新接口，通过增强型虚拟化身实现对物理世界的操控，推动构建物理元宇宙。
AI.motivation: 现有元宇宙缺乏物理交互能力，用户无法触碰、抓取或移动现实物体，限制了虚拟与现实的深度融合。
AI.method: 设计集成隐藏机械臂的虚拟化身，通过机器人手臂执行物理操作，使用户产生直接操控物体的错觉。
AI.result: 成功实现首个支持物理交互的元宇宙接口原型，验证了虚拟化身与现实物体协同操作的可行性。
AI.conclusion: Avatarm为构建支持物理交互的元宇宙提供了关键技术路径，是虚拟与现实融合的重要里程碑。

authors_text: Zhengzhe Liu,Peng Dai,Ruihui Li,Xiaojuan Qi,Chi-Wing Fu
title: DreamStone: Image as Stepping Stone for Text-Guided 3D Shape Generation
title_zh: DreamStone：图像作为文本引导 3D 形状生成的垫脚石
comment: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI)
comments: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI)
abstract: In this paper, we present a new text-guided 3D shape generation approach DreamStone that uses images as a stepping stone to bridge the gap between text and shape modalities for generating 3D shapes without requiring paired text and 3D data. The core of our approach is a two-stage feature-space alignment strategy that leverages a pre-trained single-view reconstruction (SVR) model to map CLIP features to shapes: to begin with, map the CLIP image feature to the detail-rich 3D shape space of the SVR model, then map the CLIP text feature to the 3D shape space through encouraging the CLIP-consistency between rendered images and the input text. Besides, to extend beyond the generative capability of the SVR model, we design a text-guided 3D shape stylization module that can enhance the output shapes with novel structures and textures. Further, we exploit pre-trained text-to-image diffusion models to enhance the generative diversity, fidelity, and stylization capability. Our approach is generic, flexible, and scalable, and it can be easily integrated with various SVR models to expand the generative space and improve the generative fidelity. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in terms of generative quality and consistency with the input text. Codes and models are released at https://github.com/liuzhengzhe/DreamStone-ISS.
summary: In this paper, we present a new text-guided 3D shape generation approach DreamStone that uses images as a stepping stone to bridge the gap between text and shape modalities for generating 3D shapes without requiring paired text and 3D data. The core of our approach is a two-stage feature-space alignment strategy that leverages a pre-trained single-view reconstruction (SVR) model to map CLIP features to shapes: to begin with, map the CLIP image feature to the detail-rich 3D shape space of the SVR model, then map the CLIP text feature to the 3D shape space through encouraging the CLIP-consistency between rendered images and the input text. Besides, to extend beyond the generative capability of the SVR model, we design a text-guided 3D shape stylization module that can enhance the output shapes with novel structures and textures. Further, we exploit pre-trained text-to-image diffusion models to enhance the generative diversity, fidelity, and stylization capability. Our approach is generic, flexible, and scalable, and it can be easily integrated with various SVR models to expand the generative space and improve the generative fidelity. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in terms of generative quality and consistency with the input text. Codes and models are released at https://github.com/liuzhengzhe/DreamStone-ISS.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种新的文本引导 3D 形状生成方法 DreamStone，该方法使用图像作为垫脚石来弥合文本和形状模态之间的差距，以生成 3D 形状，而无需配对文本和 3D 数据。我们方法的核心是两阶段特征空间对齐策略，利用预先训练的单视图重建 (SVR) 模型将 CLIP 特征映射到形状：首先，将 CLIP 图像特征映射到 SVR 模型的细节丰富的 3D 形状空间，然后通过鼓励渲染图像和输入文本之间的 CLIP 一致性，将 CLIP 文本特征映射到 3D 形状空间。此外，为了扩展 SVR 模型的生成能力，我们设计了一个文本引导的 3D 形状风格化模块，可以通过新颖的结构和纹理增强输出形状。此外，我们利用预先训练的文本到图像扩散模型来增强生成多样性、保真度和风格化能力。我们的方法是通用的、灵活的、可扩展的，它可以很容易地与各种SVR模型集成，以扩展生成空间并提高生成保真度。大量的实验结果表明，我们的方法在生成质量和与输入文本的一致性方面优于最先进的方法。代码和模型发布于https://github.com/liuzhengzhe/DreamStone-ISS。

AI.motivation: 现有的文本引导3D形状生成方法通常需要配对的文本和3D数据，这限制了其应用和灵活性。本文旨在提出一种无需配对数据的方法，以提升生成质量和多样性。

AI.result: 实验结果表明，该方法在生成质量和与输入文本的一致性方面优于现有最先进的方法。
AI.conclusion: DreamStone方法通用、灵活且可扩展，能够与各种SVR模型集成，扩展生成空间并提高生成保真度。

search_text: DreamStone: Image as Stepping Stone for Text-Guided 3D Shape Generation DreamStone：图像作为文本引导 3D 形状生成的垫脚石 In this paper, we present a new text-guided 3D shape generation approach DreamStone that uses images as a stepping stone to bridge the gap between text and shape modalities for generating 3D shapes without requiring paired text and 3D data. The core of our approach is a two-stage feature-space alignment strategy that leverages a pre-trained single-view reconstruction (SVR) model to map CLIP features to shapes: to begin with, map the CLIP image feature to the detail-rich 3D shape space of the SVR model, then map the CLIP text feature to the 3D shape space through encouraging the CLIP-consistency between rendered images and the input text. Besides, to extend beyond the generative capability of the SVR model, we design a text-guided 3D shape stylization module that can enhance the output shapes with novel structures and textures. Further, we exploit pre-trained text-to-image diffusion models to enhance the generative diversity, fidelity, and stylization capability. Our approach is generic, flexible, and scalable, and it can be easily integrated with various SVR models to expand the generative space and improve the generative fidelity. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that our approach outperforms the state-of-the-art methods in terms of generative quality and consistency with the input text. Codes and models are released at https://github.com/liuzhengzhe/DreamStone-ISS. 在本文中，我们提出了一种新的文本引导 3D 形状生成方法 DreamStone，该方法使用图像作为垫脚石来弥合文本和形状模态之间的差距，以生成 3D 形状，而无需配对文本和 3D 数据。我们方法的核心是两阶段特征空间对齐策略，利用预先训练的单视图重建 (SVR) 模型将 CLIP 特征映射到形状：首先，将 CLIP 图像特征映射到 SVR 模型的细节丰富的 3D 形状空间，然后通过鼓励渲染图像和输入文本之间的 CLIP 一致性，将 CLIP 文本特征映射到 3D 形状空间。此外，为了扩展 SVR 模型的生成能力，我们设计了一个文本引导的 3D 形状风格化模块，可以通过新颖的结构和纹理增强输出形状。此外，我们利用预先训练的文本到图像扩散模型来增强生成多样性、保真度和风格化能力。我们的方法是通用的、灵活的、可扩展的，它可以很容易地与各种SVR模型集成，以扩展生成空间并提高生成保真度。大量的实验结果表明，我们的方法在生成质量和与输入文本的一致性方面优于最先进的方法。代码和模型发布于https://github.com/liuzhengzhe/DreamStone-ISS。 本文提出了一种新的文本引导的3D形状生成方法DreamStone，利用图像作为中间桥梁，无需配对的文本和3D数据即可生成3D形状。 Zhengzhe Liu,Peng Dai,Ruihui Li,Xiaojuan Qi,Chi-Wing Fu cs.CV

authors_text: Shiwei Feng,Guanhong Tao,Siyuan Cheng,Guangyu Shen,Xiangzhe Xu,Yingqi Liu,Kaiyuan Zhang,Shiqing Ma,Xiangyu Zhang
title: Detecting Backdoors in Pre-trained Encoders
title_zh: 检测预训练编码器中的后门
comment: Accepted at CVPR 2023. Code is available at https://github.com/GiantSeaweed/DECREE
comments: Accepted at CVPR 2023. Code is available at https://github.com/GiantSeaweed/DECREE
abstract: Self-supervised learning in computer vision trains on unlabeled data, such as images or (image, text) pairs, to obtain an image encoder that learns high-quality embeddings for input data. Emerging backdoor attacks towards encoders expose crucial vulnerabilities of self-supervised learning, since downstream classifiers (even further trained on clean data) may inherit backdoor behaviors from encoders. Existing backdoor detection methods mainly focus on supervised learning settings and cannot handle pre-trained encoders especially when input labels are not available. In this paper, we propose DECREE, the first backdoor detection approach for pre-trained encoders, requiring neither classifier headers nor input labels. We evaluate DECREE on over 400 encoders trojaned under 3 paradigms. We show the effectiveness of our method on image encoders pre-trained on ImageNet and OpenAI's CLIP 400 million image-text pairs. Our method consistently has a high detection accuracy even if we have only limited or no access to the pre-training dataset.
summary: Self-supervised learning in computer vision trains on unlabeled data, such as images or (image, text) pairs, to obtain an image encoder that learns high-quality embeddings for input data. Emerging backdoor attacks towards encoders expose crucial vulnerabilities of self-supervised learning, since downstream classifiers (even further trained on clean data) may inherit backdoor behaviors from encoders. Existing backdoor detection methods mainly focus on supervised learning settings and cannot handle pre-trained encoders especially when input labels are not available. In this paper, we propose DECREE, the first backdoor detection approach for pre-trained encoders, requiring neither classifier headers nor input labels. We evaluate DECREE on over 400 encoders trojaned under 3 paradigms. We show the effectiveness of our method on image encoders pre-trained on ImageNet and OpenAI's CLIP 400 million image-text pairs. Our method consistently has a high detection accuracy even if we have only limited or no access to the pre-training dataset.
abstract_zh: 计算机视觉中的自监督学习对未标记的数据（例如图像或（图像，文本）对）进行训练，以获得学习输入数据的高质量嵌入的图像编码器。针对编码器的新兴后门攻击暴露了自监督学习的关键漏洞，因为下游分类器（甚至在干净数据上进一步训练）可能会继承编码器的后门行为。现有的后门检测方法主要集中在监督学习设置上，无法处理预训练的编码器，特别是在输入标签不可用时。在本文中，我们提出了 DECREE，这是第一个用于预训练编码器的后门检测方法，既不需要分类器标头，也不需要输入标签。我们对 3 种范式下的 400 多个编码器木马进行了 DECREE 评估。我们展示了我们的方法对在 ImageNet 和 OpenAI 的 CLIP 4 亿图像文本对上预训练的图像编码器的有效性。即使我们只能有限地或无法访问预训练数据集，我们的方法始终具有很高的检测精度。

AI.tldr: 提出一种无需分类器头和输入标签的预训练编码器后门检测方法DECREE
AI.motivation: 现有后门检测方法多针对监督学习，难以处理无输入标签的预训练编码器，而后门攻击可能影响下游任务，需新方法
AI.method: DECREE通过分析编码器嵌入分布和激活模式检测后门，无需分类器头或输入标签
AI.result: 在400多个受感染编码器上验证，ImageNet和CLIP模型检测准确率高，即使无预训练数据访问
AI.conclusion: DECREE有效检测预训练编码器后门，填补监督方法空白，保障自监督学习模型安全

authors_text: Niklas Paulig,Ostap Okhrin
title: Robust Path Following on Rivers Using Bootstrapped Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 使用引导强化学习在河流上进行鲁棒路径追踪
abstract: This paper develops a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-agent for navigation and control of autonomous surface vessels (ASV) on inland waterways. Spatial restrictions due to waterway geometry and the resulting challenges, such as high flow velocities or shallow banks, require controlled and precise movement of the ASV. A state-of-the-art bootstrapped Q-learning algorithm in combination with a versatile training environment generator leads to a robust and accurate rudder controller. To validate our results, we compare the path-following capabilities of the proposed approach to a vessel-specific PID controller on real-world river data from the lower- and middle Rhine, indicating that the DRL algorithm could effectively prove generalizability even in never-seen scenarios while simultaneously attaining high navigational accuracy.
summary: This paper develops a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-agent for navigation and control of autonomous surface vessels (ASV) on inland waterways. Spatial restrictions due to waterway geometry and the resulting challenges, such as high flow velocities or shallow banks, require controlled and precise movement of the ASV. A state-of-the-art bootstrapped Q-learning algorithm in combination with a versatile training environment generator leads to a robust and accurate rudder controller. To validate our results, we compare the path-following capabilities of the proposed approach to a vessel-specific PID controller on real-world river data from the lower- and middle Rhine, indicating that the DRL algorithm could effectively prove generalizability even in never-seen scenarios while simultaneously attaining high navigational accuracy.
abstract_zh: 本文开发了一种深度强化学习（DRL）代理，用于内陆水道上自主水面船舶（ASV）的导航和控制。由于水道几何形状造成的空间限制以及由此带来的挑战，例如高流速或浅岸，需要 ASV 的受控和精确运动。最先进的自举 Q 学习算法与多功能训练环境生成器相结合，形成了强大而准确的舵控制器。为了验证我们的结果，我们将所提出的方法与特定于船舶的 PID 控制器在来自莱茵河下游和中游的真实河流数据上的路径跟踪能力进行了比较，表明 DRL 算法即使在从未见过的场景中也可以有效地证明通用性，同时获得高导航精度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于引导强化学习的深度强化学习代理，用于在复杂河流环境中实现自主水面船舶的鲁棒路径跟踪控制。
AI.motivation: 内陆水道存在几何限制、高速水流和浅滩等挑战，传统控制方法难以满足自主水面船舶（ASV）对精确导航和鲁棒性的需求。
AI.method: 结合引导Q学习算法与多功能训练环境生成器，训练出适应复杂水文条件的舵控制器，并通过真实河流数据验证其性能。
AI.result: 在莱茵河实际数据测试中，DRL方法相比PID控制器展现出更优的路径跟踪精度和跨场景泛化能力，尤其在未见过的复杂水文条件下表现稳定。
AI.conclusion: 引导强化学习为ASV在动态河流环境中的自主导航提供了有效解决方案，证明了深度强化学习在复杂水道控制任务中的可行性与优势。

ai_content_json.result: 在莱茵河实际数据测试中，DRL方法相比PID控制器展现出更优的路径跟踪精度和跨场景泛化能力，尤其在未见过的复杂水文条件下表现稳定。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 引导强化学习为ASV在动态河流环境中的自主导航提供了有效解决方案，证明了深度强化学习在复杂水道控制任务中的可行性与优势。

tldr: 本文提出一种基于引导强化学习的深度强化学习代理，用于在复杂河流环境中实现自主水面船舶的鲁棒路径跟踪控制。
motivation: 内陆水道存在几何限制、高速水流和浅滩等挑战，传统控制方法难以满足自主水面船舶（ASV）对精确导航和鲁棒性的需求。
method: 结合引导Q学习算法与多功能训练环境生成器，训练出适应复杂水文条件的舵控制器，并通过真实河流数据验证其性能。
result: 在莱茵河实际数据测试中，DRL方法相比PID控制器展现出更优的路径跟踪精度和跨场景泛化能力，尤其在未见过的复杂水文条件下表现稳定。
conclusion: 引导强化学习为ASV在动态河流环境中的自主导航提供了有效解决方案，证明了深度强化学习在复杂水道控制任务中的可行性与优势。
search_text: Robust Path Following on Rivers Using Bootstrapped Reinforcement Learning 使用引导强化学习在河流上进行鲁棒路径追踪 This paper develops a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-agent for navigation and control of autonomous surface vessels (ASV) on inland waterways. Spatial restrictions due to waterway geometry and the resulting challenges, such as high flow velocities or shallow banks, require controlled and precise movement of the ASV. A state-of-the-art bootstrapped Q-learning algorithm in combination with a versatile training environment generator leads to a robust and accurate rudder controller. To validate our results, we compare the path-following capabilities of the proposed approach to a vessel-specific PID controller on real-world river data from the lower- and middle Rhine, indicating that the DRL algorithm could effectively prove generalizability even in never-seen scenarios while simultaneously attaining high navigational accuracy. 本文开发了一种深度强化学习（DRL）代理，用于内陆水道上自主水面船舶（ASV）的导航和控制。由于水道几何形状造成的空间限制以及由此带来的挑战，例如高流速或浅岸，需要 ASV 的受控和精确运动。最先进的自举 Q 学习算法与多功能训练环境生成器相结合，形成了强大而准确的舵控制器。为了验证我们的结果，我们将所提出的方法与特定于船舶的 PID 控制器在来自莱茵河下游和中游的真实河流数据上的路径跟踪能力进行了比较，表明 DRL 算法即使在从未见过的场景中也可以有效地证明通用性，同时获得高导航精度。 本文提出一种基于引导强化学习的深度强化学习代理，用于在复杂河流环境中实现自主水面船舶的鲁棒路径跟踪控制。 Niklas Paulig,Ostap Okhrin cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.RO

authors_text: Fumiaki Sato,Ryo Hachiuma,Taiki Sekii
title: Prompt-Guided Zero-Shot Anomaly Action Recognition using Pretrained Deep Skeleton Features
title_zh: 使用预训练的深度骨架特征进行提示引导的零样本异常动作识别
abstract: This study investigates unsupervised anomaly action recognition, which identifies video-level abnormal-human-behavior events in an unsupervised manner without abnormal samples, and simultaneously addresses three limitations in the conventional skeleton-based approaches: target domain-dependent DNN training, robustness against skeleton errors, and a lack of normal samples. We present a unified, user prompt-guided zero-shot learning framework using a target domain-independent skeleton feature extractor, which is pretrained on a large-scale action recognition dataset. Particularly, during the training phase using normal samples, the method models the distribution of skeleton features of the normal actions while freezing the weights of the DNNs and estimates the anomaly score using this distribution in the inference phase. Additionally, to increase robustness against skeleton errors, we introduce a DNN architecture inspired by a point cloud deep learning paradigm, which sparsely propagates the features between joints. Furthermore, to prevent the unobserved normal actions from being misidentified as abnormal actions, we incorporate a similarity score between the user prompt embeddings and skeleton features aligned in the common space into the anomaly score, which indirectly supplements normal actions. On two publicly available datasets, we conduct experiments to test the effectiveness of the proposed method with respect to abovementioned limitations.
summary: This study investigates unsupervised anomaly action recognition, which identifies video-level abnormal-human-behavior events in an unsupervised manner without abnormal samples, and simultaneously addresses three limitations in the conventional skeleton-based approaches: target domain-dependent DNN training, robustness against skeleton errors, and a lack of normal samples. We present a unified, user prompt-guided zero-shot learning framework using a target domain-independent skeleton feature extractor, which is pretrained on a large-scale action recognition dataset. Particularly, during the training phase using normal samples, the method models the distribution of skeleton features of the normal actions while freezing the weights of the DNNs and estimates the anomaly score using this distribution in the inference phase. Additionally, to increase robustness against skeleton errors, we introduce a DNN architecture inspired by a point cloud deep learning paradigm, which sparsely propagates the features between joints. Furthermore, to prevent the unobserved normal actions from being misidentified as abnormal actions, we incorporate a similarity score between the user prompt embeddings and skeleton features aligned in the common space into the anomaly score, which indirectly supplements normal actions. On two publicly available datasets, we conduct experiments to test the effectiveness of the proposed method with respect to abovementioned limitations.
abstract_zh: 本研究研究了无监督异常动作识别，即在没有异常样本的情况下以无监督方式识别视频级异常人类行为事件，同时解决了传统基于骨架的方法中的三个局限性：目标域相关的 DNN 训练、针对骨架错误的鲁棒性和正常样本的缺乏。我们提出了一个统一的、用户提示引导的零样本学习框架，使用目标域无关的骨架特征提取器，该框架在大规模动作识别数据集上进行了预训练。特别是，在使用正常样本的训练阶段，该方法对正常动作的骨架特征分布进行建模，同时冻结 DNN 的权重，并在推理阶段使用该分布来估计异常分数。此外，为了提高针对骨架错误的鲁棒性，我们引入了受点云深度学习范式启发的 DNN 架构，该架构在关节之间稀疏地传播特征。此外，为了防止未观察到的正常动作被误识别为异常动作，我们将用户提示嵌入和公共空间中对齐的骨架特征之间的相似性得分纳入异常得分，从而间接补充正常动作。在两个公开可用的数据集上，我们进行了实验来测试所提出的方法相对于上述限制的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于预训练深度骨骼特征和用户提示引导的零样本异常动作识别方法，无需异常样本即可检测视频中的异常人类行为。
AI.motivation: 传统骨骼动作识别方法存在目标领域依赖、对骨骼错误鲁棒性差及正常样本不足等局限，需设计无监督框架解决这些问题。
AI.method: 采用预训练的领域无关骨骼特征提取器，冻结DNN权重建模正常动作分布；引入点云学习启发的稀疏特征传播结构提升鲁棒性；结合用户提示嵌入与骨骼特征相似度补充正常样本信息。
AI.result: 在两个公开数据集上验证了方法对异常动作识别的有效性，显著缓解了传统方法的三大局限性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架通过预训练特征、稀疏传播结构和提示引导机制，有效提升了无监督异常动作识别的性能与泛化能力。

authors_text: Ran Yi,Haoyuan Tian,Zhihao Gu,Yu-Kun Lai,Paul L. Rosin
title: Towards Artistic Image Aesthetics Assessment: a Large-scale Dataset and a New Method
title_zh: 走向艺术形象美学评估：大规模数据集和新方法
abstract: Image aesthetics assessment (IAA) is a challenging task due to its highly subjective nature. Most of the current studies rely on large-scale datasets (e.g., AVA and AADB) to learn a general model for all kinds of photography images. However, little light has been shed on measuring the aesthetic quality of artistic images, and the existing datasets only contain relatively few artworks. Such a defect is a great obstacle to the aesthetic assessment of artistic images. To fill the gap in the field of artistic image aesthetics assessment (AIAA), we first introduce a large-scale AIAA dataset: Boldbrush Artistic Image Dataset (BAID), which consists of 60,337 artistic images covering various art forms, with more than 360,000 votes from online users. We then propose a new method, SAAN (Style-specific Art Assessment Network), which can effectively extract and utilize style-specific and generic aesthetic information to evaluate artistic images. Experiments demonstrate that our proposed approach outperforms existing IAA methods on the proposed BAID dataset according to quantitative comparisons. We believe the proposed dataset and method can serve as a foundation for future AIAA works and inspire more research in this field. Dataset and code are available at: https://github.com/Dreemurr-T/BAID.git
summary: Image aesthetics assessment (IAA) is a challenging task due to its highly subjective nature. Most of the current studies rely on large-scale datasets (e.g., AVA and AADB) to learn a general model for all kinds of photography images. However, little light has been shed on measuring the aesthetic quality of artistic images, and the existing datasets only contain relatively few artworks. Such a defect is a great obstacle to the aesthetic assessment of artistic images. To fill the gap in the field of artistic image aesthetics assessment (AIAA), we first introduce a large-scale AIAA dataset: Boldbrush Artistic Image Dataset (BAID), which consists of 60,337 artistic images covering various art forms, with more than 360,000 votes from online users. We then propose a new method, SAAN (Style-specific Art Assessment Network), which can effectively extract and utilize style-specific and generic aesthetic information to evaluate artistic images. Experiments demonstrate that our proposed approach outperforms existing IAA methods on the proposed BAID dataset according to quantitative comparisons. We believe the proposed dataset and method can serve as a foundation for future AIAA works and inspire more research in this field. Dataset and code are available at: https://github.com/Dreemurr-T/BAID.git
abstract_zh: 图像美学评估（IAA）因其高度主观性而成为一项具有挑战性的任务。目前的大多数研究依赖于大规模数据集（例如AVA和AADB）来学习各种摄影图像的通用模型。然而，对于衡量艺术图像的美学质量却知之甚少，现有的数据集只包含相对较少的艺术品。这样的缺陷对于艺术形象的审美评价来说是一个很大的障碍。为了填补艺术图像美学评估（AIAA）领域的空白，我们首先引入了一个大规模的AIAA数据集：Boldbrush艺术图像数据集（BAID），该数据集由涵盖各种艺术形式的60,337张艺术图像组成，有超过360,000张在线用户投票。然后，我们提出了一种新方法——SAAN（特定风格艺术评估网络），它可以有效地提取和利用特定风格和通用的审美信息来评估艺术图像。实验表明，根据定量比较，我们提出的方法在所提出的 BAID 数据集上优于现有的 IAA 方法。我们相信所提出的数据集和方法可以作为未来 AIAA 工作的基础，并激发该领域的更多研究。数据集和代码可在以下位置获取：https://github.com/Dreemurr-T/BAID.git

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个大规模艺术图像美学评估数据集BAID和一种新型评估方法SAAN，以解决艺术图像美学评估领域数据不足和方法缺失的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有数据集（如AVA、AADB）缺乏艺术图像样本，且难以准确评估艺术作品的美学质量，制约了艺术图像美学评估（AIAA）领域的发展。
AI.method: 构建包含60,337幅艺术图像和360,000个用户评分的BAID数据集，并设计SAAN网络，通过提取风格特异性与通用美学特征实现艺术图像评估。
AI.result: SAAN在BAID数据集上优于现有图像美学评估方法，且数据集与代码已开源，为后续研究提供基础。
AI.conclusion: 本文为艺术图像美学评估提供了首个大规模数据集和有效方法，有望推动该领域的进一步研究。

Such a defect is a great obstacle to the aesthetic assessment of artistic images. To fill the gap in the field of artistic image aesthetics assessment (AIAA), we first introduce a large-scale AIAA dataset: Boldbrush Artistic Image Dataset (BAID), which consists of 60,337 artistic images covering various art forms, with more than 360,000 votes from online users. We then propose a new method, SAAN (Style-specific Art Assessment Network), which can effectively extract and utilize style-specific and generic aesthetic information to evaluate artistic images. Experiments demonstrate that our proposed approach outperforms existing IAA methods on the proposed BAID dataset according to quantitative comparisons. We believe the proposed dataset and method can serve as a foundation for future AIAA works and inspire more research in this field. Dataset and code are available at: https://github.com/Dreemurr-T/BAID.git 图像美学评估（IAA）因其高度主观性而成为一项具有挑战性的任务。目前的大多数研究依赖于大规模数据集（例如AVA和AADB）来学习各种摄影图像的通用模型。然而，对于衡量艺术图像的美学质量却知之甚少，现有的数据集只包含相对较少的艺术品。这样的缺陷对于艺术形象的审美评价来说是一个很大的障碍。为了填补艺术图像美学评估（AIAA）领域的空白，我们首先引入了一个大规模的AIAA数据集：Boldbrush艺术图像数据集（BAID），该数据集由涵盖各种艺术形式的60,337张艺术图像组成，有超过360,000张在线用户投票。然后，我们提出了一种新方法——SAAN（特定风格艺术评估网络），它可以有效地提取和利用特定风格和通用的审美信息来评估艺术图像。实验表明，根据定量比较，我们提出的方法在所提出的 BAID 数据集上优于现有的 IAA 方法。我们相信所提出的数据集和方法可以作为未来 AIAA 工作的基础，并激发该领域的更多研究。数据集和代码可在以下位置获取：https://github.com/Dreemurr-T/BAID.git 本文提出一个大规模艺术图像美学评估数据集BAID和一种新型评估方法SAAN，以解决艺术图像美学评估领域数据不足和方法缺失的问题。 Ran Yi,Haoyuan Tian,Zhihao Gu,Yu-Kun Lai,Paul L. Rosin cs.CV

authors_text: Victor P. Seidel,Christoph Riedl
title: How creative versus technical constraints affect individual learning in an online innovation community
title_zh: 创意与技术限制如何影响在线创新社区中的个人学习
abstract: Online innovation communities allow for a search for novel solutions within a design space bounded by constraints. Past research has focused on the effect of creative constraints on individual projects, but less is known about how constraints affect learning from repeated design submissions and the effect of the technical constraints that are integral to online platforms. How do creative versus technical constraints affect individual learning in exploring a design space in online communities? We analyzed ten years of data from an online innovation community that crowdsourced 136,989 design submissions from 33,813 individuals. We leveraged data from two types of design contests-creatively constrained and unconstrained-running in parallel on the platform, and we evaluated a natural experiment where a platform change reduced technical constraints. We find that creative constraints lead to high rates of learning only if technical constraints are sufficiently relaxed. Our findings have implications for the management of creative design work and the downstream effects of the technical constraints of the information systems that support online innovation communities.
summary: Online innovation communities allow for a search for novel solutions within a design space bounded by constraints. Past research has focused on the effect of creative constraints on individual projects, but less is known about how constraints affect learning from repeated design submissions and the effect of the technical constraints that are integral to online platforms. How do creative versus technical constraints affect individual learning in exploring a design space in online communities? We analyzed ten years of data from an online innovation community that crowdsourced 136,989 design submissions from 33,813 individuals. We leveraged data from two types of design contests-creatively constrained and unconstrained-running in parallel on the platform, and we evaluated a natural experiment where a platform change reduced technical constraints. We find that creative constraints lead to high rates of learning only if technical constraints are sufficiently relaxed. Our findings have implications for the management of creative design work and the downstream effects of the technical constraints of the information systems that support online innovation communities.
abstract_zh: 在线创新社区允许在受约束的设计空间内寻找新颖的解决方案。过去的研究主要集中在创意限制对单个项目的影响，但对于限制如何影响重复设计提交的学习以及在线平台不可或缺的技术限制的影响，人们知之甚少。创意与技术限制如何影响在线社区中探索设计空间的个人学习？我们分析了在线创新社区十年来的数据，该社区众包了 33,813 名个人提交的 136,989 份设计作品。我们利用了在平台上并行运行的两种类型的设计竞赛（创造性约束和无约束）的数据，并评估了一个自然实验，其中平台更改减少了技术约束。我们发现，只有在技术限制充分放松的情况下，创造性限制才会导致高学习率。我们的研究结果对创意设计工作的管理以及支持在线创新社区的信息系统的技术限制的下游影响具有影响。

AI.tldr: 探讨创意约束与技术约束对在线创新社区中个人学习的影响
AI.motivation: 现有研究多关注创意约束对项目的影响，但缺乏对重复设计提交中学习效果及技术约束影响的了解
AI.method: 分析十年间在线社区的136,989个设计提交数据，对比创意约束与无约束比赛，并评估平台技术约束变化的自然实验
AI.result: 创意约束仅在技术约束充分放松时显著提升学习率
AI.conclusion: 技术约束的调整对创意设计管理及在线社区学习效果具有重要影响

authors_text: Yunhao Chen,Yunjie Zhu,Zihui Yan,Jianlu Shen,Zhen Ren,Yifan Huang
title: Data Augmentation for Environmental Sound Classification Using Diffusion Probabilistic Model with Top-k Selection Discriminator
title_zh: 使用具有 Top-k 选择鉴别器的扩散概率模型进行环境声音分类的数据增强
abstract: Despite consistent advancement in powerful deep learning techniques in recent years, large amounts of training data are still necessary for the models to avoid overfitting. Synthetic datasets using generative adversarial networks (GAN) have recently been generated to overcome this problem. Nevertheless, despite advancements, GAN-based methods are usually hard to train or fail to generate high-quality data samples. In this paper, we propose an environmental sound classification augmentation technique based on the diffusion probabilistic model with DPM-Solver$++$ for fast sampling. In addition, to ensure the quality of the generated spectrograms, we train a top-k selection discriminator on the dataset. According to the experiment results, the synthesized spectrograms have similar features to the original dataset and can significantly increase the classification accuracy of different state-of-the-art models compared with traditional data augmentation techniques. The public code is available on https://github.com/JNAIC/DPMs-for-Audio-Data-Augmentation.
summary: Despite consistent advancement in powerful deep learning techniques in recent years, large amounts of training data are still necessary for the models to avoid overfitting. Synthetic datasets using generative adversarial networks (GAN) have recently been generated to overcome this problem. Nevertheless, despite advancements, GAN-based methods are usually hard to train or fail to generate high-quality data samples. In this paper, we propose an environmental sound classification augmentation technique based on the diffusion probabilistic model with DPM-Solver$++$ for fast sampling. In addition, to ensure the quality of the generated spectrograms, we train a top-k selection discriminator on the dataset. According to the experiment results, the synthesized spectrograms have similar features to the original dataset and can significantly increase the classification accuracy of different state-of-the-art models compared with traditional data augmentation techniques. The public code is available on https://github.com/JNAIC/DPMs-for-Audio-Data-Augmentation.
abstract_zh: 尽管近年来强大的深度学习技术不断进步，但模型仍然需要大量的训练数据来避免过度拟合。最近生成了使用生成对抗网络（GAN）的合成数据集来克服这个问题。然而，尽管取得了进步，基于 GAN 的方法通常很难训练或无法生成高质量的数据样本。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于扩散概率模型和 DPM-Solver$++$ 的环境声音分类增强技术，用于快速采样。此外，为了确保生成的频谱图的质量，我们在数据集上训练了一个 top-k 选择鉴别器。根据实验结果，合成的谱图与原始数据集具有相似的特征，与传统的数据增强技术相比，可以显着提高不同最先进模型的分类精度。公共代码可在 https://github.com/JNAIC/DPMs-for-Audio-Data-Augmentation 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于扩散概率模型和Top-k选择鉴别器的环境声音分类数据增强方法。
AI.motivation: 传统深度学习模型需大量训练数据避免过拟合，而GAN等生成方法存在训练困难和生成质量低的问题。
AI.method: 采用扩散概率模型结合DPM-Solver++实现快速采样，并通过Top-k选择鉴别器筛选高质量生成频谱图。
AI.result: 生成数据与原始数据特征相似，显著提升多种先进模型的分类准确率，优于传统数据增强技术。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决环境声分类数据不足问题，开源代码可促进相关研究应用。

authors_text: Pinaki Nath Chowdhury,Ayan Kumar Bhunia,Aneeshan Sain,Subhadeep Koley,Tao Xiang,Yi-Zhe Song
title: What Can Human Sketches Do for Object Detection?
title_zh: 人体草图可以为物体检测做什么？
comment: Best Paper Finalist (Top 12 Best Papers). Presented in special single-track plenary sessions to all attendees in Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2023. Updated an error in Fig.3 (from Softmax to Cross Entropy). Thanks to the community for pointing it out
comments: Best Paper Finalist (Top 12 Best Papers). Presented in special single-track plenary sessions to all attendees in Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR), 2023. Updated an error in Fig.3 (from Softmax to Cross Entropy). Thanks to the community for pointing it out
abstract: Sketches are highly expressive, inherently capturing subjective and fine-grained visual cues. The exploration of such innate properties of human sketches has, however, been limited to that of image retrieval. In this paper, for the first time, we cultivate the expressiveness of sketches but for the fundamental vision task of object detection. The end result is a sketch-enabled object detection framework that detects based on what \textit{you} sketch -- \textit{that} ``zebra'' (e.g., one that is eating the grass) in a herd of zebras (instance-aware detection), and only the \textit{part} (e.g., ``head" of a ``zebra") that you desire (part-aware detection). We further dictate that our model works without (i) knowing which category to expect at testing (zero-shot) and (ii) not requiring additional bounding boxes (as per fully supervised) and class labels (as per weakly supervised). Instead of devising a model from the ground up, we show an intuitive synergy between foundation models (e.g., CLIP) and existing sketch models build for sketch-based image retrieval (SBIR), which can already elegantly solve the task -- CLIP to provide model generalisation, and SBIR to bridge the (sketch$\rightarrow$photo) gap. In particular, we first perform independent prompting on both sketch and photo branches of an SBIR model to build highly generalisable sketch and photo encoders on the back of the generalisation ability of CLIP. We then devise a training paradigm to adapt the learned encoders for object detection, such that the region embeddings of detected boxes are aligned with the sketch and photo embeddings from SBIR. Evaluating our framework on standard object detection datasets like PASCAL-VOC and MS-COCO outperforms both supervised (SOD) and weakly-supervised object detectors (WSOD) on zero-shot setups. Project Page: \url{https://pinakinathc.github.io/sketch-detect}

Evaluating our framework on standard object detection datasets like PASCAL-VOC and MS-COCO outperforms both supervised (SOD) and weakly-supervised object detectors (WSOD) on zero-shot setups. abstract_zh: 草图具有很强的表现力，本质上捕捉主观和细粒度的视觉线索。然而，对人类草图这种固有属性的探索仅限于图像检索。在本文中，我们首次培养草图的表现力，但用于目标检测的基本视觉任务。最终结果是一个支持草图的对象检测框架，它基于 \textit{you} 草图 - \textit{that} 一群斑马中的“斑马”（例如，正在吃草的斑马）（实例感知检测）进行检测，并且仅检测您想要的 \textit{part} （例如，“斑马”的“头”）（部分感知检测）。我们进一步规定，我们的模型无需（i）知道测试时期望哪个类别（零样本）和（ii）不需要额外的边界框（根据完全监督）和类标签（根据弱监督）即可工作。我们没有从头开始设计模型，而是展示了基础模型（例如 CLIP）和为基于草图的图像检索（SBIR）而构建的现有草图模型之间的直观协同作用，这已经可以优雅地解决任务——CLIP 提供模型泛化，而 SBIR 则弥合（sketch$\rightarrow$photo）差距。特别是，我们首先对 SBIR 模型的草图和照片分支进行独立提示，以在 CLIP 的泛化能力的基础上构建高度泛化的草图和照片编码器。然后，我们设计了一种训练范例，使学习到的编码器适应对象检测，使得检测到的框的区域嵌入与 SBIR 的草图和照片嵌入对齐。在 PASCAL-VOC 和 MS-COCO 等标准目标检测数据集上评估我们的框架，在零样本设置上优于监督 (SOD) 和弱监督目标检测器 (WSOD)。项目页面：\url{https://pinakinathc.github.io/sketch-detect}

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于人类草图的物体检测框架，实现了无需预设类别和额外标注的实例感知与部件感知检测。
AI.motivation: 传统草图研究局限于图像检索，未探索其在基础视觉任务（如物体检测）中的潜力，且现有方法依赖类别先验或额外标注。
AI.method: 结合CLIP等基础模型与草图图像检索（SBIR）模型，通过独立提示构建通用编码器，并对齐检测区域嵌入与SBIR嵌入以实现检测。
AI.result: 在PASCAL-VOC和MS-COCO数据集上，零样本设置下优于监督和弱监督检测器。
AI.conclusion: 草图的表达性可有效用于物体检测，无需额外数据即可实现通用化检测，为视觉任务提供新方向。

search_text: What Can Human Sketches Do for Object Detection? 人体草图可以为物体检测做什么？ Sketches are highly expressive, inherently capturing subjective and fine-grained visual cues. The exploration of such innate properties of human sketches has, however, been limited to that of image retrieval. In this paper, for the first time, we cultivate the expressiveness of sketches but for the fundamental vision task of object detection. The end result is a sketch-enabled object detection framework that detects based on what \textit{you} sketch -- \textit{that} ``zebra'' (e.g., one that is eating the grass) in a herd of zebras (instance-aware detection), and only the \textit{part} (e.g., ``head" of a ``zebra") that you desire (part-aware detection). We further dictate that our model works without (i) knowing which category to expect at testing (zero-shot) and (ii) not requiring additional bounding boxes (as per fully supervised) and class labels (as per weakly supervised). Instead of devising a model from the ground up, we show an intuitive synergy between foundation models (e.g., CLIP) and existing sketch models build for sketch-based image retrieval (SBIR), which can already elegantly solve the task -- CLIP to provide model generalisation, and SBIR to bridge the (sketch$\rightarrow$photo) gap. In particular, we first perform independent prompting on both sketch and photo branches of an SBIR model to build highly generalisable sketch and photo encoders on the back of the generalisation ability of CLIP. We then devise a training paradigm to adapt the learned encoders for object detection, such that the region embeddings of detected boxes are aligned with the sketch and photo embeddings from SBIR. Evaluating our framework on standard object detection datasets like PASCAL-VOC and MS-COCO outperforms both supervised (SOD) and weakly-supervised object detectors (WSOD) on zero-shot setups. authors_text: Mohammad Rezaei,Farnaz Farahanipad,Alex Dillhoff,Vassilis Athitsos
title: Pushing the Envelope for Depth-Based Semi-Supervised 3D Hand Pose Estimation with Consistency Training
title_zh: 通过一致性训练突破基于深度的半监督 3D 手势估计的极限
abstract: Despite the significant progress that depth-based 3D hand pose estimation methods have made in recent years, they still require a large amount of labeled training data to achieve high accuracy. However, collecting such data is both costly and time-consuming. To tackle this issue, we propose a semi-supervised method to significantly reduce the dependence on labeled training data. The proposed method consists of two identical networks trained jointly: a teacher network and a student network. The teacher network is trained using both the available labeled and unlabeled samples. It leverages the unlabeled samples via a loss formulation that encourages estimation equivariance under a set of affine transformations. The student network is trained using the unlabeled samples with their pseudo-labels provided by the teacher network. For inference at test time, only the student network is used. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art semi-supervised methods by large margins.
summary: Despite the significant progress that depth-based 3D hand pose estimation methods have made in recent years, they still require a large amount of labeled training data to achieve high accuracy. However, collecting such data is both costly and time-consuming. To tackle this issue, we propose a semi-supervised method to significantly reduce the dependence on labeled training data. The proposed method consists of two identical networks trained jointly: a teacher network and a student network. The teacher network is trained using both the available labeled and unlabeled samples. It leverages the unlabeled samples via a loss formulation that encourages estimation equivariance under a set of affine transformations. The student network is trained using the unlabeled samples with their pseudo-labels provided by the teacher network. For inference at test time, only the student network is used. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art semi-supervised methods by large margins.
abstract_zh: 尽管近年来基于深度的 3D 手势估计方法取得了重大进展，但它们仍然需要大量标记的训练数据才能实现高精度。然而，收集此类数据既昂贵又耗时。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种半监督方法来显着减少对标记训练数据的依赖。所提出的方法由两个共同训练的相同网络组成：教师网络和学生网络。教师网络使用可用的标记和未标记样本进行训练。它通过损失公式来利用未标记的样本，该损失公式鼓励在一组仿射变换下估计等方差。使用教师网络提供的未标记样本及其伪标签来训练学生网络。对于测试时的推理，仅使用学生网络。大量的实验表明，所提出的方法大大优于最先进的半监督方法。

AI.tldr: 提出了一种基于深度图像的半监督3D手部姿态估计方法，通过一致性训练减少对标注数据的依赖。
AI.motivation: 传统深度图像3D手部姿态估计方法依赖大量标注数据，而标注成本高且耗时，需探索减少标注数据依赖的解决方案。
AI.method: 构建教师网络与学生网络联合训练框架，教师网络利用标注与未标注数据并通过仿射变换损失增强鲁棒性，学生网络基于教师网络生成的伪标签进行训练，测试时仅使用学生网络。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在多个基准数据集上显著优于现有最先进的半监督3D手部姿态估计方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过一致性训练有效利用未标注数据，验证了半监督学习在减少标注依赖性的同时提升模型性能的可行性。

However, collecting such data is both costly and time-consuming. To tackle this issue, we propose a semi-supervised method to significantly reduce the dependence on labeled training data. It leverages the unlabeled samples via a loss formulation that encourages estimation equivariance under a set of affine transformations. The student network is trained using the unlabeled samples with their pseudo-labels provided by the teacher network. For inference at test time, only the student network is used. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the state-of-the-art semi-supervised methods by large margins. 尽管近年来基于深度的 3D 手势估计方法取得了重大进展，但它们仍然需要大量标记的训练数据才能实现高精度。然而，收集此类数据既昂贵又耗时。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种半监督方法来显着减少对标记训练数据的依赖。所提出的方法由两个共同训练的相同网络组成：教师网络和学生网络。教师网络使用可用的标记和未标记样本进行训练。它通过损失公式来利用未标记的样本，该损失公式鼓励在一组仿射变换下估计等方差。使用教师网络提供的未标记样本及其伪标签来训练学生网络。对于测试时的推理，仅使用学生网络。大量的实验表明，所提出的方法大大优于最先进的半监督方法。 提出了一种基于深度图像的半监督3D手部姿态估计方法，通过一致性训练减少对标注数据的依赖。 Mohammad Rezaei,Farnaz Farahanipad,Alex Dillhoff,Vassilis Athitsos cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Zhikang Liu,Yiming Zhou,Yuansheng Xu,Zilei Wang
title: SimpleNet: A Simple Network for Image Anomaly Detection and Localization
title_zh: SimpleNet：用于图像异常检测和定位的简单网络
abstract: We propose a simple and application-friendly network (called SimpleNet) for detecting and localizing anomalies. SimpleNet consists of four components: (1) a pre-trained Feature Extractor that generates local features, (2) a shallow Feature Adapter that transfers local features towards target domain, (3) a simple Anomaly Feature Generator that counterfeits anomaly features by adding Gaussian noise to normal features, and (4) a binary Anomaly Discriminator that distinguishes anomaly features from normal features. During inference, the Anomaly Feature Generator would be discarded. Our approach is based on three intuitions. First, transforming pre-trained features to target-oriented features helps avoid domain bias. Second, generating synthetic anomalies in feature space is more effective, as defects may not have much commonality in the image space. Third, a simple discriminator is much efficient and practical. In spite of simplicity, SimpleNet outperforms previous methods quantitatively and qualitatively. On the MVTec AD benchmark, SimpleNet achieves an anomaly detection AUROC of 99.6%, reducing the error by 55.5% compared to the next best performing model. Furthermore, SimpleNet is faster than existing methods, with a high frame rate of 77 FPS on a 3080ti GPU. Additionally, SimpleNet demonstrates significant improvements in performance on the One-Class Novelty Detection task. Code: https://github.com/DonaldRR/SimpleNet.
summary: We propose a simple and application-friendly network (called SimpleNet) for detecting and localizing anomalies. SimpleNet consists of four components: (1) a pre-trained Feature Extractor that generates local features, (2) a shallow Feature Adapter that transfers local features towards target domain, (3) a simple Anomaly Feature Generator that counterfeits anomaly features by adding Gaussian noise to normal features, and (4) a binary Anomaly Discriminator that distinguishes anomaly features from normal features. During inference, the Anomaly Feature Generator would be discarded. Our approach is based on three intuitions. First, transforming pre-trained features to target-oriented features helps avoid domain bias. Second, generating synthetic anomalies in feature space is more effective, as defects may not have much commonality in the image space. Third, a simple discriminator is much efficient and practical. In spite of simplicity, SimpleNet outperforms previous methods quantitatively and qualitatively. On the MVTec AD benchmark, SimpleNet achieves an anomaly detection AUROC of 99.6%, reducing the error by 55.5% compared to the next best performing model. Furthermore, SimpleNet is faster than existing methods, with a high frame rate of 77 FPS on a 3080ti GPU. Additionally, SimpleNet demonstrates significant improvements in performance on the One-Class Novelty Detection task. Code: https://github.com/DonaldRR/SimpleNet.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个简单且应用程序友好的网络（称为 SimpleNet）来检测和定位异常。 SimpleNet 由四个组件组成：（1）一个生成局部特征的预训练特征提取器，（2）一个将局部特征转移到目标域的浅层特征适配器，（3）一个通过向正常特征添加高斯噪声来伪造异常特征的简单异常特征生成器，以及（4）一个区分异常特征和正常特征的二元异常鉴别器。在推理过程中，异常特征生成器将被丢弃。我们的方法基于三个直觉。首先，将预先训练的特征转换为面向目标的特征有助于避免领域偏差。其次，在特征空间中生成合成异常更为有效，因为缺陷在图像空间中可能没有太多共性。第三，简单的判别器非常高效且实用。尽管简单，SimpleNet 在数量和质量上都优于以前的方法。在 MVTec AD 基准上，SimpleNet 实现了 99.6% 的异常检测 AUROC，与排名第二的最佳性能模型相比，误差减少了 55.5%。此外，SimpleNet 比现有方法更快，在 3080ti GPU 上具有 77 FPS 的高帧速率。此外，SimpleNet 在单类新颖性检测任务上表现出显着的性能改进。代码：https://github.com/DonaldRR/SimpleNet。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为SimpleNet的简单网络，用于图像异常检测与定位。
AI.motivation: 为避免领域偏差、提升合成异常生成效果并提高效率，设计简单高效的网络结构。
AI.method: SimpleNet包含四部分：预训练特征提取器、浅层特征适配器、异常特征生成器（通过添加高斯噪声生成异常特征）和二元异常判别器。推理时移除生成器。
AI.result: 在MVTec AD基准测试中，SimpleNet达到99.6% AUROC，较最优模型误差降低55.5%，GPU帧率达77 FPS，并在单类新颖性检测任务中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: SimpleNet以简洁结构实现高精度与高效率，验证了简单设计在实际应用中的有效性。

search_text: SimpleNet: A Simple Network for Image Anomaly Detection and Localization SimpleNet：用于图像异常检测和定位的简单网络 We propose a simple and application-friendly network (called SimpleNet) for detecting and localizing anomalies. SimpleNet consists of four components: (1) a pre-trained Feature Extractor that generates local features, (2) a shallow Feature Adapter that transfers local features towards target domain, (3) a simple Anomaly Feature Generator that counterfeits anomaly features by adding Gaussian noise to normal features, and (4) a binary Anomaly Discriminator that distinguishes anomaly features from normal features. During inference, the Anomaly Feature Generator would be discarded. Our approach is based on three intuitions. First, transforming pre-trained features to target-oriented features helps avoid domain bias. Second, generating synthetic anomalies in feature space is more effective, as defects may not have much commonality in the image space. Third, a simple discriminator is much efficient and practical. In spite of simplicity, SimpleNet outperforms previous methods quantitatively and qualitatively. On the MVTec AD benchmark, SimpleNet achieves an anomaly detection AUROC of 99.6%, reducing the error by 55.5% compared to the next best performing model. Furthermore, SimpleNet is faster than existing methods, with a high frame rate of 77 FPS on a 3080ti GPU. Additionally, SimpleNet demonstrates significant improvements in performance on the One-Class Novelty Detection task. Code: https://github.com/DonaldRR/SimpleNet. 我们提出了一个简单且应用程序友好的网络（称为 SimpleNet）来检测和定位异常。 SimpleNet 由四个组件组成：（1）一个生成局部特征的预训练特征提取器，（2）一个将局部特征转移到目标域的浅层特征适配器，（3）一个通过向正常特征添加高斯噪声来伪造异常特征的简单异常特征生成器，以及（4）一个区分异常特征和正常特征的二元异常鉴别器。在推理过程中，异常特征生成器将被丢弃。我们的方法基于三个直觉。首先，将预先训练的特征转换为面向目标的特征有助于避免领域偏差。其次，在特征空间中生成合成异常更为有效，因为缺陷在图像空间中可能没有太多共性。第三，简单的判别器非常高效且实用。尽管简单，SimpleNet 在数量和质量上都优于以前的方法。在 MVTec AD 基准上，SimpleNet 实现了 99.6% 的异常检测 AUROC，与排名第二的最佳性能模型相比，误差减少了 55.5%。此外，SimpleNet 比现有方法更快，在 3080ti GPU 上具有 77 FPS 的高帧速率。此外，SimpleNet 在单类新颖性检测任务上表现出显着的性能改进。代码：https://github.com/DonaldRR/SimpleNet。 本文提出一种名为SimpleNet的简单网络，用于图像异常检测与定位。 Zhikang Liu,Yiming Zhou,Yuansheng Xu,Zilei Wang cs.CV

authors_text: Srinath Tankasala,Long Chen,Andreas Stolcke,Anirudh Raju,Qianli Deng,Chander Chandak,Aparna Khare,Roland Maas,Venkatesh Ravichandran
title: Cross-utterance ASR Rescoring with Graph-based Label Propagation
title_zh: 使用基于图的标签传播进行跨话语 ASR 重新评分
abstract: We propose a novel approach for ASR N-best hypothesis rescoring with graph-based label propagation by leveraging cross-utterance acoustic similarity. In contrast to conventional neural language model (LM) based ASR rescoring/reranking models, our approach focuses on acoustic information and conducts the rescoring collaboratively among utterances, instead of individually. Experiments on the VCTK dataset demonstrate that our approach consistently improves ASR performance, as well as fairness across speaker groups with different accents. Our approach provides a low-cost solution for mitigating the majoritarian bias of ASR systems, without the need to train new domain- or accent-specific models.
summary: We propose a novel approach for ASR N-best hypothesis rescoring with graph-based label propagation by leveraging cross-utterance acoustic similarity. In contrast to conventional neural language model (LM) based ASR rescoring/reranking models, our approach focuses on acoustic information and conducts the rescoring collaboratively among utterances, instead of individually. Experiments on the VCTK dataset demonstrate that our approach consistently improves ASR performance, as well as fairness across speaker groups with different accents. Our approach provides a low-cost solution for mitigating the majoritarian bias of ASR systems, without the need to train new domain- or accent-specific models.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种利用交叉话语声学相似性通过基于图的标签传播进行 ASR N 最佳假设重新评分的新方法。与基于传统神经语言模型 (LM) 的 ASR 重新评分/重新排名模型相比，我们的方法侧重于声学信息，并在话语之间协作进行重新评分，而不是单独进行。 VCTK 数据集上的实验表明，我们的方法持续提高了 ASR 性能，以及不同口音的说话者群体之间的公平性。我们的方法提供了一种低成本的解决方案，可以减轻 ASR 系统的多数偏见，而无需训练新的特定领域或特定口音的模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于图的标签传播方法，通过跨utterance声学相似性协作重打分，提升ASR系统的公平性并减少多数群体偏差。
AI.motivation: 传统基于神经语言模型的ASR重打分方法难以有效利用跨utterance声学信息，导致系统对不同口音说话人存在偏差，需低成本解决方案。
AI.method: 构建声学相似性图，通过图基标签传播算法在跨utterance间协作传播可靠标签，实现N-best假设的联合重打分。
AI.result: 在VCTK数据集上验证，该方法提升ASR性能并改善不同口音群体的公平性，无需训练新模型即可缓解多数群体偏差。
AI.conclusion: 图基标签传播方法为ASR系统提供了一种低成本、高效的跨utterance协作重打分方案，有效提升模型公平性与性能。

authors_text: Tianrui Qin,Xitong Gao,Juanjuan Zhao,Kejiang Ye,Cheng-Zhong Xu
title: Learning the Unlearnable: Adversarial Augmentations Suppress Unlearnable Example Attacks
title_zh: 学习不可学习的东西：对抗性增强抑制不可学习的示例攻击
comment: UEraser introduces adversarial augmentations to suppress unlearnable example attacks and outperforms current defenses
comments: UEraser introduces adversarial augmentations to suppress unlearnable example attacks and outperforms current defenses
abstract: Unlearnable example attacks are data poisoning techniques that can be used to safeguard public data against unauthorized use for training deep learning models. These methods add stealthy perturbations to the original image, thereby making it difficult for deep learning models to learn from these training data effectively. Current research suggests that adversarial training can, to a certain degree, mitigate the impact of unlearnable example attacks, while common data augmentation methods are not effective against such poisons. Adversarial training, however, demands considerable computational resources and can result in non-trivial accuracy loss. In this paper, we introduce the UEraser method, which outperforms current defenses against different types of state-of-the-art unlearnable example attacks through a combination of effective data augmentation policies and loss-maximizing adversarial augmentations. In stark contrast to the current SOTA adversarial training methods, UEraser uses adversarial augmentations, which extends beyond the confines of $ \ell_p $ perturbation budget assumed by current unlearning attacks and defenses. It also helps to improve the model's generalization ability, thus protecting against accuracy loss. UEraser wipes out the unlearning effect with error-maximizing data augmentations, thus restoring trained model accuracies. Interestingly, UEraser-Lite, a fast variant without adversarial augmentations, is also highly effective in preserving clean accuracies. On challenging unlearnable CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and ImageNet-subset datasets produced with various attacks, it achieves results that are comparable to those obtained during clean training. We also demonstrate its efficacy against possible adaptive attacks. Our code is open source and available to the deep learning community: https://github.com/lafeat/ueraser.
summary: Unlearnable example attacks are data poisoning techniques that can be used to safeguard public data against unauthorized use for training deep learning models. These methods add stealthy perturbations to the original image, thereby making it difficult for deep learning models to learn from these training data effectively. Current research suggests that adversarial training can, to a certain degree, mitigate the impact of unlearnable example attacks, while common data augmentation methods are not effective against such poisons. Adversarial training, however, demands considerable computational resources and can result in non-trivial accuracy loss. In this paper, we introduce the UEraser method, which outperforms current defenses against different types of state-of-the-art unlearnable example attacks through a combination of effective data augmentation policies and loss-maximizing adversarial augmentations. In stark contrast to the current SOTA adversarial training methods, UEraser uses adversarial augmentations, which extends beyond the confines of $ \ell_p $ perturbation budget assumed by current unlearning attacks and defenses. It also helps to improve the model's generalization ability, thus protecting against accuracy loss. UEraser wipes out the unlearning effect with error-maximizing data augmentations, thus restoring trained model accuracies. Interestingly, UEraser-Lite, a fast variant without adversarial augmentations, is also highly effective in preserving clean accuracies. On challenging unlearnable CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and ImageNet-subset datasets produced with various attacks, it achieves results that are comparable to those obtained during clean training. We also demonstrate its efficacy against possible adaptive attacks. Our code is open source and available to the deep learning community: https://github.com/lafeat/ueraser.
abstract_zh: 不可学习的示例攻击是数据中毒技术，可用于保护公共数据免遭未经授权的使用来训练深度学习模型。这些方法给原始图像添加了隐秘的扰动，从而使深度学习模型难以有效地从这些训练数据中学习。目前的研究表明，对抗性训练可以在一定程度上减轻不可学习的示例攻击的影响，而常见的数据增强方法对此类毒药无效。然而，对抗性训练需要大量的计算资源，并且可能导致不小的准确性损失。在本文中，我们介绍了 UEraser 方法，该方法通过结合有效的数据增强策略和损失最大化的对抗性增强，优于当前针对不同类型的最先进的不可学习示例攻击的防御。与当前的 SOTA 对抗训练方法形成鲜明对比，UEraser 使用对抗增强，超出了当前不学习攻击和防御假设的 $ \ell_p $ 扰动预算的范围。它还有助于提高模型的泛化能力，从而防止准确性损失。 UEraser 通过误差最大化的数据增强消除了遗忘效应，从而恢复了训练模型的准确性。有趣的是，UEraser-Lite，一种没有对抗性增强的快速变体，在保持干净的准确性方面也非常有效。在挑战通过各种攻击生成的不可学习的 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、SVHN 和 ImageNet 子集数据集时，它取得了与干净训练期间获得的结果相当的结果。我们还展示了它针对可能的自适应攻击的功效。我们的代码是开源的，可供深度学习社区使用：https://github.com/lafeat/ueraser。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为UEraser的新方法，通过结合有效数据增强策略和损失最大化对抗增强，以抵御不可学习示例攻击。
AI.motivation: 现有防御方法（如对抗训练）计算成本高且会导致精度损失，而常规数据增强对这类攻击无效，因此需要更高效且有效的防御方案。
AI.method: UEraser结合数据增强策略与超越ℓ_p扰动预算的对抗增强，通过误差最大化数据增强消除不可学习效果；其轻量版UEraser-Lite无需对抗增强即可保持高精度。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、SVHN和ImageNet子集等数据集上，UEraser的性能接近干净训练结果，并有效抵御了自适应攻击。
AI.conclusion: UEraser通过对抗增强显著提升了模型泛化能力，在防御不可学习示例攻击时兼顾效率与精度，代码已开源。

conclusion: UEraser通过对抗增强显著提升了模型泛化能力，在防御不可学习示例攻击时兼顾效率与精度，代码已开源。
search_text: Learning the Unlearnable: Adversarial Augmentations Suppress Unlearnable Example Attacks 学习不可学习的东西：对抗性增强抑制不可学习的示例攻击 Unlearnable example attacks are data poisoning techniques that can be used to safeguard public data against unauthorized use for training deep learning models. These methods add stealthy perturbations to the original image, thereby making it difficult for deep learning models to learn from these training data effectively. Current research suggests that adversarial training can, to a certain degree, mitigate the impact of unlearnable example attacks, while common data augmentation methods are not effective against such poisons. Adversarial training, however, demands considerable computational resources and can result in non-trivial accuracy loss. In this paper, we introduce the UEraser method, which outperforms current defenses against different types of state-of-the-art unlearnable example attacks through a combination of effective data augmentation policies and loss-maximizing adversarial augmentations. In stark contrast to the current SOTA adversarial training methods, UEraser uses adversarial augmentations, which extends beyond the confines of $ \ell_p $ perturbation budget assumed by current unlearning attacks and defenses. It also helps to improve the model's generalization ability, thus protecting against accuracy loss. UEraser wipes out the unlearning effect with error-maximizing data augmentations, thus restoring trained model accuracies. Interestingly, UEraser-Lite, a fast variant without adversarial augmentations, is also highly effective in preserving clean accuracies. On challenging unlearnable CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, SVHN, and ImageNet-subset datasets produced with various attacks, it achieves results that are comparable to those obtained during clean training. We also demonstrate its efficacy against possible adaptive attacks. Our code is open source and available to the deep learning community: https://github.com/lafeat/ueraser. 不可学习的示例攻击是数据中毒技术，可用于保护公共数据免遭未经授权的使用来训练深度学习模型。这些方法给原始图像添加了隐秘的扰动，从而使深度学习模型难以有效地从这些训练数据中学习。目前的研究表明，对抗性训练可以在一定程度上减轻不可学习的示例攻击的影响，而常见的数据增强方法对此类毒药无效。然而，对抗性训练需要大量的计算资源，并且可能导致不小的准确性损失。在本文中，我们介绍了 UEraser 方法，该方法通过结合有效的数据增强策略和损失最大化的对抗性增强，优于当前针对不同类型的最先进的不可学习示例攻击的防御。与当前的 SOTA 对抗训练方法形成鲜明对比，UEraser 使用对抗增强，超出了当前不学习攻击和防御假设的 $ \ell_p $ 扰动预算的范围。它还有助于提高模型的泛化能力，从而防止准确性损失。 UEraser 通过误差最大化的数据增强消除了遗忘效应，从而恢复了训练模型的准确性。有趣的是，UEraser-Lite，一种没有对抗性增强的快速变体，在保持干净的准确性方面也非常有效。在挑战通过各种攻击生成的不可学习的 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100、SVHN 和 ImageNet 子集数据集时，它取得了与干净训练期间获得的结果相当的结果。我们还展示了它针对可能的自适应攻击的功效。我们的代码是开源的，可供深度学习社区使用：https://github.com/lafeat/ueraser。 本文提出了一种名为UEraser的新方法，通过结合有效数据增强策略和损失最大化对抗增强，以抵御不可学习示例攻击。 Tianrui Qin,Xitong Gao,Juanjuan Zhao,Kejiang Ye,Cheng-Zhong Xu cs.LG,cs.CR,cs.CV

authors_text: Zehan Zheng,Danni Wu,Ruisi Lu,Fan Lu,Guang Chen,Changjun Jiang
title: NeuralPCI: Spatio-temporal Neural Field for 3D Point Cloud Multi-frame Non-linear Interpolation
title_zh: NeuralPCI：3D 点云多帧非线性插值的时空神经场
comment: Accepted by CVPR 2023. Project Page: https://dyfcalid.github.io/NeuralPCI
comments: Accepted by CVPR 2023. Project Page: https://dyfcalid.github.io/NeuralPCI
abstract: In recent years, there has been a significant increase in focus on the interpolation task of computer vision. Despite the tremendous advancement of video interpolation, point cloud interpolation remains insufficiently explored. Meanwhile, the existence of numerous nonlinear large motions in real-world scenarios makes the point cloud interpolation task more challenging. In light of these issues, we present NeuralPCI: an end-to-end 4D spatio-temporal Neural field for 3D Point Cloud Interpolation, which implicitly integrates multi-frame information to handle nonlinear large motions for both indoor and outdoor scenarios. Furthermore, we construct a new multi-frame point cloud interpolation dataset called NL-Drive for large nonlinear motions in autonomous driving scenes to better demonstrate the superiority of our method. Ultimately, NeuralPCI achieves state-of-the-art performance on both DHB (Dynamic Human Bodies) and NL-Drive datasets. Beyond the interpolation task, our method can be naturally extended to point cloud extrapolation, morphing, and auto-labeling, which indicates its substantial potential in other domains. Codes are available at https://github.com/ispc-lab/NeuralPCI.
summary: In recent years, there has been a significant increase in focus on the interpolation task of computer vision. Despite the tremendous advancement of video interpolation, point cloud interpolation remains insufficiently explored. Meanwhile, the existence of numerous nonlinear large motions in real-world scenarios makes the point cloud interpolation task more challenging. In light of these issues, we present NeuralPCI: an end-to-end 4D spatio-temporal Neural field for 3D Point Cloud Interpolation, which implicitly integrates multi-frame information to handle nonlinear large motions for both indoor and outdoor scenarios. Furthermore, we construct a new multi-frame point cloud interpolation dataset called NL-Drive for large nonlinear motions in autonomous driving scenes to better demonstrate the superiority of our method. Ultimately, NeuralPCI achieves state-of-the-art performance on both DHB (Dynamic Human Bodies) and NL-Drive datasets. Beyond the interpolation task, our method can be naturally extended to point cloud extrapolation, morphing, and auto-labeling, which indicates its substantial potential in other domains. Codes are available at https://github.com/ispc-lab/NeuralPCI.
abstract_zh: 近年来，人们对计算机视觉插值任务的关注显着增加。尽管视频插值取得了巨大进步，但点云插值仍然没有得到充分的探索。同时，现实场景中大量非线性大运动的存在使得点云插值任务更具挑战性。鉴于这些问题，我们提出了 NeuralPCI：一种用于 3D 点云插值的端到端 4D 时空神经场，它隐式集成多帧信息来处理室内和室外场景的非线性大运动。此外，我们构建了一个新的多帧点云插值数据集NL-Drive，用于自动驾驶场景中的大型非线性运动，以更好地证明我们方法的优越性。最终，NeuralPCI 在 DHB（动态人体）和 NL-Drive 数据集上实现了最先进的性能。除了插值任务之外，我们的方法可以自然地扩展到点云外推、变形和自动标记，这表明其在其他领域的巨大潜力。代码可在 https://github.com/ispc-lab/NeuralPCI 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出NeuralPCI方法，解决3D点云多帧非线性插值问题，适用于室内外复杂场景。
AI.motivation: 现有研究在点云插值领域进展不足，尤其难以处理真实场景中的非线性大运动，缺乏有效方法和数据集支持。
AI.method: 构建端到端4D时空神经场模型，整合多帧点云信息，设计NL-Drive数据集用于自动驾驶场景的非线性运动验证。
AI.result: 在DHB和NL-Drive数据集上达到SOTA性能，方法可扩展至点云外推、变形及自动标注任务。
AI.conclusion: NeuralPCI有效解决了点云非线性插值难题，展现跨领域应用潜力，代码已开源促进后续研究。

authors_text: Tae Soo Kim,Arghya Sarkar,Yoonjoo Lee,Minsuk Chang,Juho Kim
title: LMCanvas: Object-Oriented Interaction to Personalize Large Language Model-Powered Writing Environments
title_zh: LMCanvas：面向对象的交互来个性化大型语言模型驱动的写作环境
comment: Accepted to CHI 2023 Workshop on Generative AI and HCI
comments: Accepted to CHI 2023 Workshop on Generative AI and HCI
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can enhance writing by automating or supporting specific tasks in writers' workflows (e.g., paraphrasing, creating analogies). Leveraging this capability, a collection of interfaces have been developed that provide LLM-powered tools for specific writing tasks. However, these interfaces provide limited support for writers to create personal tools for their own unique tasks, and may not comprehensively fulfill a writer's needs -- requiring them to continuously switch between interfaces during writing. In this work, we envision LMCanvas, an interface that enables writers to create their own LLM-powered writing tools and arrange their personal writing environment by interacting with "blocks" in a canvas. In this interface, users can create text blocks to encapsulate writing and LLM prompts, model blocks for model parameter configurations, and connect these to create pipeline blocks that output generations. In this workshop paper, we discuss the design for LMCanvas and our plans to develop this concept.
summary: Large language models (LLMs) can enhance writing by automating or supporting specific tasks in writers' workflows (e.g., paraphrasing, creating analogies). Leveraging this capability, a collection of interfaces have been developed that provide LLM-powered tools for specific writing tasks. However, these interfaces provide limited support for writers to create personal tools for their own unique tasks, and may not comprehensively fulfill a writer's needs -- requiring them to continuously switch between interfaces during writing. In this work, we envision LMCanvas, an interface that enables writers to create their own LLM-powered writing tools and arrange their personal writing environment by interacting with "blocks" in a canvas. In this interface, users can create text blocks to encapsulate writing and LLM prompts, model blocks for model parameter configurations, and connect these to create pipeline blocks that output generations. In this workshop paper, we discuss the design for LMCanvas and our plans to develop this concept.
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 可以通过自动化或支持作者工作流程中的特定任务（例如，释义、创建类比）来增强写作能力。利用这一功能，开发了一系列接口，为特定的写作任务提供由法学硕士支持的工具。然而，这些界面为作家为自己独特的任务创建个人工具提供的支持有限，并且可能无法全面满足作家的需求——要求他们在写作过程中不断地在界面之间切换。在这项工作中，我们设想了 LMCanvas，这是一个界面，使作者能够创建自己的 LLM 支持的写作工具，并通过与画布中的“块”交互来安排他们的个人写作环境。在此界面中，用户可以创建文本块来封装书写和LLM提示，模型块用于模型参数配置，并将它们连接起来以创建输出生成的管​​道块。在这篇研讨会论文中，我们讨论了 LMCanvas 的设计以及我们开发这一概念的计划。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LMCanvas，一种通过面向对象交互方式让用户自定义大型语言模型驱动的写作环境的界面设计。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM写作工具缺乏对用户个性化任务的支持，导致用户需频繁切换不同界面，无法满足多样化写作需求。
AI.method: 设计LMCanvas界面，允许用户通过拖拽和连接文本块、模型配置块等模块，构建个性化写作工具流水线。
AI.result: 提出了LMCanvas的设计框架及开发计划，但尚未进行完整实现与实证测试。
AI.conclusion: LMCanvas有望通过模块化交互解决现有工具的碎片化问题，为个性化写作提供统一环境。

authors_text: Xuechen Guo,Wenhao Hu,Chiming Ni,Wenhao Chai,Shiyan Li,Gaoang Wang
title: Blind Inpainting with Object-aware Discrimination for Artificial Marker Removal
title_zh: 通过对象感知辨别进行盲修复以实现人工标记去除
abstract: Medical images often incorporate doctor-added markers that can hinder AI-based diagnosis. This issue highlights the need of inpainting techniques to restore the corrupted visual contents. However, existing methods require manual mask annotation as input, limiting the application scenarios. In this paper, we propose a novel blind inpainting method that automatically reconstructs visual contents within the corrupted regions without mask input as guidance. Our model includes a blind reconstruction network and an object-aware discriminator for adversarial training. The reconstruction network contains two branches that predict corrupted regions in images and simultaneously restore the missing visual contents. Leveraging the potent recognition capability of a dense object detector, the object-aware discriminator ensures markers undetectable after inpainting. Thus, the restored images closely resemble the clean ones. We evaluate our method on three datasets of various medical imaging modalities, confirming better performance over other state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Medical images often incorporate doctor-added markers that can hinder AI-based diagnosis. This issue highlights the need of inpainting techniques to restore the corrupted visual contents. However, existing methods require manual mask annotation as input, limiting the application scenarios. In this paper, we propose a novel blind inpainting method that automatically reconstructs visual contents within the corrupted regions without mask input as guidance. Our model includes a blind reconstruction network and an object-aware discriminator for adversarial training. The reconstruction network contains two branches that predict corrupted regions in images and simultaneously restore the missing visual contents. Leveraging the potent recognition capability of a dense object detector, the object-aware discriminator ensures markers undetectable after inpainting. Thus, the restored images closely resemble the clean ones. We evaluate our method on three datasets of various medical imaging modalities, confirming better performance over other state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 医学图像通常包含医生添加的标记，这些标记可能会阻碍基于人工智能的诊断。这个问题凸显了修复技术来恢复损坏的视觉内容的必要性。然而，现有方法需要手动掩模注释作为输入，限制了应用场景。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的盲修复方法，该方法可以自动重建损坏区域内的视觉内容，而无需掩模输入作为指导。我们的模型包括盲重建网络和用于对抗训练的对象感知鉴别器。重建网络包含两个分支，可预测图像中的损坏区域并同时恢复丢失的视觉内容。利用密集物体检测器的强大识别能力，物体感知鉴别器确保修复后标记无法被检测到。因此，恢复的图像与干净的图像非常相似。我们在不同医学成像模式的三个数据集上评估我们的方法，确认其性能优于其他最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需人工标注掩码的盲修复方法，用于自动去除医学图像中的人工标记。
AI.motivation: 医学图像中的人工标记会干扰AI诊断，现有修复方法依赖手动掩码输入，限制了实际应用。
AI.method: 设计包含盲重建网络和对象感知判别器的模型，通过对抗训练实现无掩码输入的自动修复，利用密集检测器确保标记不可检测。
AI.result: 在三个医学影像数据集上验证，修复效果优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法能有效生成高质量修复图像，适用于不同模态的医学影像，提升AI诊断的准确性。
